received amplification stacks additively with the [[Global_Passive_Abilities#Healing_reduction_from_damage|Global passive]] healing received reduction. * Biotic Grenade's healing is unaffected by healing received amplification, but it can be affected by healing received reduction. * The regeneration of [[Hit points#Shields|shields]] is treated as a form of healing, meaning allies regenerate their shields 50% faster and enemies cannot regenerate their shields until the effect expires. * Has no effect on overhealth. * The projectile can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage and healing dealt, if the projectile passes through it. raw.ability details: * Damage per second: 136 while active ** B.O.B can deal a total of 1326 damage over the duration. * B.O.B. appears slightly to the right of Ashe and lands 5 meters in front of her, then charges in the direction Ashe is looking at. Once B.O.B. bumps into an enemy or a wall, or travels 65 meters, B.O.B. halts and begins shooting nearby enemies like a turret. ** If B.O.B. bumps into an enemy (or enemies), B.O.B. inflicts 120 damage to them and flings them into the air. ** If a charging B.O.B. collides with [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]], [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]], or another charging B.O.B., both participants get [[Crowd Control#Knock Down|knocked down]] for 1.7 seconds. ** B.O.B. automatically uses [[Symmetra]]'s [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]] if it's in his path. * Unlike other turrets, B.O.B. has spread, meaning if you are far away enough, not every single bullet will land, this is most noticeable at his max range. * B.O.B. prefers to target enemies that Ashe damages with her rifle. ** Otherwise, B.O.B.'s targeting most likely behaves the same as [[Torbjorn]]'s turret. * B.O.B. is treated as an additional player for most effects, such as contesting/capturing objectives or [[Wrecking Ball]]'s [[File:Adaptive_shield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Adaptive Shield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Adaptive Shield|Adaptive Shield]]. ** B.O.B. can be healed, buffed (e.g. [[File:Ability-ana4.png|sub|link=Ana#Nano Boost|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ana#Nano Boost|Nano Boost]]), and debuffed (e.g. [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]]). *** If B.O.B. is hacked, B.O.B. becomes inactive for 3 seconds. ** Damaging or healing B.O.B. does not give [[Ultimate ability|ultimate charge]], but damage boosting as [[Mercy]] does. * B.O.B.'s charge cannot be blocked by [[barrier]]s, [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]] [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] or boosted by [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], but the shots fired by him interact normally with all of the mentioned. * B.O.B.'s charge counts as a [[movement ability]], and is interrupted by [[rooted]] and [[hindered]]. * Damage amplification buffs on Ashe do not affect B.O.B., but he can be directly boosted by them. * Damage from B.O.B. applies the healing reduction from the [[File:Role Damage Circle.svg|sub|link=Roles#Role: Damage|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roles#Role: Damage|Role: Damage]] passive, as if he were a [[Damage]] hero. * B.O.B. does not receive the healing reduction from damage role passive when hit by a damage hero. * B.O.B. has the [[File:Role Tank Circle.svg|sub|link=Roles#Role: Tank|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roles#Role: Tank|Role: Tank]] passive and is considered a Tank in most regards. ** This includes receiving reduced critical/headshot damage, reduced knockback, reduced [[File:Ability-ana2.png|sub|link=Ana#Sleep Dart|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ana#Sleep Dart|Sleep Dart]] duration, additional [[Hit points]] in Role Queue, etc.

raw.official description: Toss a device that prevents allies from dying. The device can be destroyed.
raw.heal: Any deficit from 20% HP
raw.cast time: 0.8 second cast animation
raw.ability keywords: area of effect;;cylindrical::deployable;;strong projectile::ignore barrier;;target ally
raw.ability details: * Prevents the [[HP]] of Baptiste and his allies from going below 20%. It even prevents environmental death, but healing the HP lost this way will not give [[Ultimate ability|ultimate charge]]. * Heroes below the minimum HP threshold will instantly be healed to the threshold when entering the field. [[File:Ability-ana3.png|sub|link=Ana#Biotic Grenade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ana#Biotic Grenade|Biotic Grenade]] can amplify or block this; if the healing is blocked, Immortality Field will still prevent all incoming damage. * The device can be thrown and bounced off walls. The arc is identical to that of Biotic Launcher's alternate fire, except Immortality Field collides with the skybox. * Both the thrown device and the field ignore [[barrier]]s but can be blocked by terrain and terrain-like [[objects]]. * [[Sombra]] can [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]] the device instantly destroying Immortality Field. * Placing a [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] on the device destroys it. * The device can be placed on and lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] if the latter was deployed first. * When the device expires, the field will linger for 0.5 seconds. If the generator is destroyed, the field will still linger, but not as long. * Although the ability has a casting animation, the device is spawned the moment the button is pressed, and therefore cannot be cancelled mid-cast by a stun or hack. * Projectile bounces off of breakables and cannot deploy even on ones with a walkable top surface.

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
raw.official description: Project a matrix that doubles the damage and healing of allied projectiles.
raw.ability details: * Damage per second: ~258.2 while firing (Biotic Launcher) * Healing per second: 133.2 - 199.8 (indirect - direct) while firing (Biotic Launcher) * Before being deployed, the orientation can be changed by pressing the ultimate key again. * [[Damage amplification|Amplifies]] hitscan and projectile attacks alike, coming from any direction. ** This includes [[abilities]] that travel as a projectile but deal damage/healing in an AoE or as a DoT/HoT, such as [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]], [[File:Ability-ashe2.png|sub|link=Ashe#Dynamite|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#Dynamite|Dynamite]] and [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Orb.png|sub|link=Moira#Biotic Orb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Orb|Biotic Orb]] (note that it does not change the total damage/healing for Biotic Orb, just the rate of damage/healing), as long as (and only if) the projectile passes through. ** This does ''not'' include Brigitte's Repair Pack and Zenyatta's Orb of Harmony/Discord. * You can tell if your projectile has been successfully amplified, as there is an associated sound effect and visual effect. * Cannot amplify the damage of melee-based projectiles such as [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]], [[File:Whip Shot.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Whip Shot|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Whip Shot|Whip Shot]] or [[File:Ability Binding Chain.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Binding Chain|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Binding Chain|Binding Chain]]. * Amplification Matrix does not actually double final damage/healing, but increases base damage dealt by an additional 100%. When combined with other amplification abilities, they are added together. For example, Amplification Matrix (+100%) and [[Mercy]]'s Damage Boost (+25%) will result in +125% damage (225% damage overall). * If Amplification Matrix is placed on a temporary surface, such as [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], it will remain suspended in midair after the surface disappears. Conversely, if it is placed on a moving surface, such as the [[payload]], it will move with that surface. * Can be destroyed by [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]]. * Destroys fences and other breakables that collide with it.

raw.official description: Press E to rapidly heal yourself.
raw.heal: 90 per second
raw.ability details: * Self-Repair uses a resource meter. It takes 3.3 seconds of continuous healing from full to completely deplete. ** Resource meter starts recharging 1 second after the ability is deactivated. ** It takes 6.7 seconds to recharge 100% meter (7.7s including delay). ** At least ~20% meter must be available to activate. If the meter is completely empty this takes 2.5 seconds to reach. ** If Bastion is at full health, the meter will not deplete, but regeneration is still paused if the ability is still active. * Can be activated even while not damaged, automatically healing Bastion if he takes damage. No resource will be consumed while Bastion is full HP. * Can be used in [[File:Ability-bastion2.png|middle|link=Bastion#Configuration: Assault|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Bastion#Configuration: Assault|Configuration: Assault]] but not during [[File:Artillery_Icon.png|sub|link=Bastion#Configuration: Artillery|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Bastion#Configuration: Artillery|Configuration: Artillery]]. * Self-Repair is a [[channeled]] ability; it is interrupted if Bastion uses other abilities or fires or he receives [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]] [[status effect|status]].

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
raw.official description: Dealing damage to enemies heals nearby allies.
raw.heal: 15 per second
raw.ability details:
* Triggered by [[Rocket Flail]], [[Whip Shot]], or [[Shield Bash]].
* If triggered, there is a 1 second cooldown before it can be triggered again by [[Rocket Flail]], but can be instantly triggered by [[Whip Shot]], or [[Shield Bash]].
** Note that 1 second is longer than the rate of fire (0.6 seconds) for [[Rocket Flail]], meaning that when spamming Flail, not every hit will trigger Inspire.
* Does not stack with itself. Instead, the duration resets.
* The Inspire effect generates a pulse that applies a heal-over-time status effect to allies in range. '"`UNIQ--ref-0000005B-QINU`"'
** This includes a subtle visible yellow pulse effect.
** Does not apply to allies behind enemy barriers or surfaces that block line of sight.
** The pulse is centered around a fixed location where Brigitte was when she dealt damage, and does not move with her.
** When the pulse hits an ally, they gain the heal-over-time effect, and will continue healing them after they leave the inspire range.
** This effect is similar to Baptise's [[Regenerative Burst]], and unlike Lucio's [[Crossfade]] aura.
* Does not trigger from hitting most non-enemy targets such as Symmetra's [[Sentry Turret]]. However, it can be triggered by hitting Wrecking Ball's [[Minefield]].
* [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] counts as an enemy and can also be hit to trigger Inspire.

heal.raw: 15 per second

raw.official description: Shield Bash grants 30% increased movement speed for 2 seconds. Inspire's healing is instant when activated by Shield Bash.
raw.ability details: * Shield Bash hits no longer reset the duration of an active Inspire when this perk is selected. * Only triggers once regardless of how many enemies were hit by Shield Bash during [[File:Rally.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Rally|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Rally|Rally]]. *Heals 60 for allies within inspire's range

raw.official description: Gain armor, empower Barrier Shield and provide extra health to nearby allies.
raw.ability details: * [[Overhealth]] per second: 30 on allies * Overhealth lasts up to 30 seconds, even if the ally leaves its radius or Brigitte is eliminated. ** The timer starts when the ally first receives overhealth from Rally. However, if the ally loses all overhealth from the Rally, the timer will start over when they start gaining it again. ** At the end of the timer, overhealth starts to decay quickly. * During Rally, Brigitte's barrier abilities are enhanced: ** [[File:Barrier Shield.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Barrier Shield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Barrier Shield|Barrier Shield]] becomes wider and its current and maximum health is increased to 700. ** [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]] impacts multiple enemies and stuns all enemies hit for 0.75 seconds. * Rally can be activated any time, interrupting Brigitte's primary fire and any ability she is currently using. * Activating Rally resets the cooldown of [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]] and restores the shield even if it was destroyed. * When activated, Brigitte instantly gains movement speed and additional [[armor]] on top of her current health pool for the duration of Rally. ** This armor can be restored by healing, but it does not regenerate by itself. ** Brigitte herself does not gain overhealth over time. ** Rally's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

raw.ability details: * Replaced in Season 11 by reworked [[File:Magnetic_Grenade.png|sub|link=Cassidy#Flashbang|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Cassidy#Flashbang|Flashbang]]. * The grenade has a travel time limit which corresponds to ~37.5m distance along the grenade's trajectory. * While in air, the grenade locks on to the first hero that enters 1.3 meter radius of the projectile. Once locked on, the grenade will continuously chase them regardless of range or until it reaches its homing or maximum travel time limit. ** The grenade will follow locked heroes through movement abilities, unless the ability causes the hero to phase out, which disjoints the grenade. ** The grenade will explode if fails to reach the hero it was locked on to within the aforementioned time limit. * The grenade will stick to enemies and surfaces upon contact. The stuck grenade explodes after 1.25 seconds, dealing damage in an area. ** The enemy hero that is stuck by the grenade becomes "[[hindered]]", interrupting and preventing movement abilities for the duration. See [[Movement speed#Movement abilities]] for a list of affected abilities. ** The grenade can be unstuck by [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]], removing the hindered status. * The projectile can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]] and [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but a stuck grenade and the splash are unaffected. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. Splash damage alone going through is not amplified.

abilities.name: Deadeye

raw.shot type: [[File:Icon-ability.tacticalvisor.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Hitscan|Hitscan ]] Hitscan
raw.official description: Face off against your enemies. Press Q to lock on, then Q or [[File:Key mouse 1.png|20x20px|link=]] to fire.
raw.damage:
150 per second (during warning)
300 per second (remaining duration)
raw.fire rate: 8.93 shots/s
raw.cast time:
0.7 + 0.3 seconds (when fired)
0.3 + 0.4 seconds (when canceled)
raw.ability details: * Activating Deadeye instantly replenishes Peacekeeper's ammo. * Deadeye can be canceled using Secondary Fire ([[File:Key mouse 2.png|20x20px|link=]]). * Deadeye is a [[channeled]] ability; getting [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], immediately ends the ability without firing. ** Cassidy cannot jump, crouch or use other abilities while channeling Deadeye. * After Deadeye has been active for 2 seconds (warning time), its charge rate is increased and its movement speed penalty and damage reduction fade to their minimum values over the next 1.4 seconds. * If the duration runs out, Deadeye ends without firing. * Deadeye can only lock on to heroes, [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] and Baptiste's [[File:Baptiste-ability3.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Immortality Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Immortality Field|Immortality Field]]. * The targeting area of Deadeye is a 105° wide 3D cone. The player's field-of-view (FOV) settings does not affect this area, meaning that Deadeye may be able to target enemies outside of view (especially vertically) or vice versa, depending on the setting. * Despite the visuals in-game, the shots are actually aimed at a point at the target's chest instead of their head.'"`UNIQ--ref-00000063-QINU`"' ** If the line of sight to this point is obstructed or it is outside the targeting area, the charging is paused and continues from the same amount when the line of sight is restored. * The lock on is removed and any accumulated damage is reset if the target becomes [[invisible]]. * Deadeye cannot be fired unless there is at least one target with unobstructed line of sight within the targeting area. * [[Barrier]]s and invulnerability states ([[phased out]], [[invincible]]) can negate/block the shots, but they do not prevent locking on, pause charging or prevent the hero from being fired at. ** [[Venture]]'s Burrow prevents lock on and pauses the charging. ** Other [[objects]] and players in front of the intended target can also block the shots. * The damage relative to the target's health is displayed as a closing red circle. If the accumulated damage is above an enemy's remaining health, a skull will appear. ** The indicator tracks the location of any enemy that has been locked on to through walls as long as the target has not become [[invisible]]. ** The indicator takes into account any [[Damage modification|damage mitigations and amplifications]], excluding [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]]. * In a normal game (and without modifiers like Mercy's damage amplify), the maximum possible damage per target is 1,800. * While unlikely in a normal game scenario, Deadeye can lock on to and shoot at more than 6 targets simultaneously. ** Ammo remaining in Peacekeeper's cylinder is not relevant as Deadeye will keep firing even if it runs out of ammo.

abilities.name: Panopticon

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile
raw.official description: Fire a hard-light barrier that imprisons enemies and detonates on expiration.
raw.pradius:
1.5 meters (globule)
0.2 meters (direct hit damage)
0.2 meters (matrix interaction)
raw.ability details: * The barrier(s) completely surround the affected target(s) in a spherical design. * Each barrier can be damaged and destroyed. Allied heroes can help destroy the barrier. ** A destroyed barrier will not detonate. ** A damaged barrier will do less damage when detonating. * The barrier acts and interacts as a typical [[Barrier]] for every ability, completely surrounding targets hit. This means: ** Sombra's [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] can destroy the barrier(s). ** Certain abilities that do not pierce barriers (such as [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]] and [[File:Ability-Lucio4.png|sub|link=Lúcio#Sound Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lúcio#Sound Barrier|Sound Barrier]]) are blocked by the barrier. However, they can still affect others if they enter the encased barrier. * Panopticon can encase [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] (though if he keeps shooting, he can break the barrier in 3.3 seconds), it can also contain [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], but only if the barrier is on the mech before the D.Va activates the ultimate. * While encased, [[Zarya]] cannot use her [[File:Ability-zarya2.png|sub|link=Zarya#Particle Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Particle Barrier|Particle Barrier]] and allies can't gained [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Projected Barrier]] protection. * The barrier cannot be cleansed and will persist if the target enters or exits a [[Phased out|phasing]] ability (such as [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]] or [[File:Ability-tracer3.png|sub|link=Tracer#Recall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Recall|Recall]]). ** The implosion damage can however be avoided while [[Phased out|Phased]] or [[Invulnerable]]. ** The only known way to directly cleanse the barrier is by [[D.Va]], who in pilot form can use [[File:Ability-dva6.png|sub|link=D.Va#Call Mech|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Call Mech|Call Mech]] to completely remove it.

raw.official description: Enemies hit by Seismic Slam are slowed by 40% for 2 seconds.
Hitting an enemy into a wall with Rocket Punch reloads all Hand Cannon and overfills ammo by 2.
Power Block absorbs projectiles for the first 0.8 seconds of its duration.
raw.ability details: * The absorption functions similarly to [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]] and [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], fully negating the projectile before it hits. Melee, AoE and beam attacks and certain projectiles, like [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|sub|link=Sigma#Accretion|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Accretion|Accretion]] and [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]], cannot be absorbed. * Absorbed projectiles' damage charges empowered Rocket Punch an equivalent amount to Power Block. * Any form of damage that cannot be absorbed still receives the baseline 80% mitigation from front. * The absorption area extends slightly forward, allowing Doomfist to absorb projectiles that would normally not hit him.

raw.official description: Hitting 3 or more enemies with Seismic Slam empowers Rocket Punch.
raw.ability details: * ''Removed in Season 18.''

raw.ability details:
*Max overhealth gained to 250
*200 (6v6)

raw.official description: Dealing damage with abilities creates temporary overhealth.
raw.ability details: * The total amount of [[overhealth]] gained through both methods is capped at +200 overhealth. * After 3 seconds, the overhealth starts decaying at 3 health per second. Dealing more ability damage will reset this condition. * Overhealth is only generated if the ability damages an enemy player. ** E.g. Hitting [[Zenyatta]] during [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]] or a construct such as [[Torbjörn|Torbjörn]]'s [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]] does not grant any overhealth.

raw.official description: Leap forward and smash the ground.
raw.ability details: * Seismic Slam features a micro-lock that will fully stop a target's movement if they are airborne, but will do virtually nothing to a grounded target. * Seismic Slam can climb both up & down surfaces within its vertical range, similar to [[File:Ability-reinhardt5.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Earthshatter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Earthshatter|Earthshatter]]. * Doomfist's momentum is retained if the ability is cancelled by Sleep Dart or Hack. * Seismic Slam can be canceled by reactivating the ability or activating any other ability. * Destroys fences and other breakables Doomfist collides with while leaping.

abilities.name: Power Block

raw.official description: Protect yourself from frontal attacks. Blocking heavy damage empowers Rocket Punch.
raw.ability details: * After mitigating 100 or more damage, the cooldown of [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]] is immediately reset and the next Rocket Punch is empowered. ** If Doomfist is already Empowered, blocking 100 or more damage with Power Block will not reset the cooldown of Rocket Punch. ** The damage requirement is based on the damage mitigated rather than the raw incoming damage, therefore at least 133 incoming damage is required to empower a punch, as Power Block provides an 75% reduction (100 damage mitigated ÷ 75% = 133.3 raw incoming damage). * Power Block is a [[channeled]] ability; the ability is interrupted when Doomfist is affected by [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], including subsidiary effects. ** The impact damage from the instance that applied the effect will still be blocked with the exception of another Doomfist's Rocket Punch. * Damage is blocked if the horizontal angle between the direction Doomfist is facing and the direction from the impact location to its source is less than ~53 degrees. ** Doomfists's vertical view angle does not affect blocking, however his head hitbox may be exposed from some angles, allowing critical hits. * Effectiveness extends to area abilities and splash damage from projectiles. Damage is generally blocked when facing the center of the AoE. ** Damage from [[File:Ability-hanzo4.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Dragonstrike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Dragonstrike|Dragonstrike]] and [[File:Ability-torbjorn5.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Molten Core|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Molten Core|Molten Core]] is blocked regardless of facing direction. ** Does not reduce damage from [[Sombra]]'s [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] or Sigma's [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] (incl. both lift and slam). * Power Block mitigates delayed or triggered detonation damage from stuck projectiles and debuffs, regardless of the direction Doomfist is facing. ** Following abilities are affected: [[File:A-36_Icon.png|sub|link=Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|A-36 Tactical Grenade]], [[File:STICKY BOMBS.png|sub|link=Echo#Sticky Bombs|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Echo#Sticky Bombs|Sticky Bombs]], [[File:Ability-tracer4.png|sub|link=Tracer#Pulse Bomb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Pulse Bomb|Pulse Bomb]] and [[File:Captive_sun_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Captive Sun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Captive Sun|Captive Sun]]. * Does not affect [[damage over time]] effects from abilities. * Power Block can be canceled by reactivating the ability, activating any other ability, or pressing primary fire.

abilities.name: Rocket Punch

raw.shot type:
[[File:Quick_melee.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Melee|Melee ]] Melee
[[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Hold to charge then release to launch forward and knock an enemy back. Damage increases if the enemy hits a wall.
raw.damage:
25 - 75 (base, impact damage)
10 - 40 (base, wall slam)
38 - 113 (empowered, impact damage)
15 - 60 (empowered, wall slam)
raw.cast time:
0.2 seconds (min)
1 seconds (full charge)
1.6 second (max)
0.4 seconds (fire recovery)
raw.duration:
0.5 seconds (max. travel time)
0.5 seconds (stun, punch impact)
0.15 seconds (stun, wall slam)
0.15 - 0.6 seconds (stun, empowered wall slam)
20 seconds (Empowered Rocket Punch max duration)
raw.ability details: * In addition to damage, charge also affects the distance the punch will travel and the distance of the knockback. * The punch impact hitbox is roughly the same size as Doomfist. * If Rocket Punch connects, it will also apply its effects to all targets in a small area behind the punched target. This is called 'Blastback'. ** The following [[deployable]]s are applicable punch targets to trigger the Blastback. [[Mei]]'s [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[Torbjörn|Torbjörn]]'s [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]], [[Baptiste]]'s [[File:Baptiste-ability3.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Immortality Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Immortality Field|Immortality Field]], [[Illari]]'s [[File:Healing_pylon_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Healing Pylon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Healing Pylon|Healing Pylon]], [[Lifeweaver]]'s [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] and [[Hazard]]'s [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]]. * Rocket Punch briefly stuns enemies on initial impact and again for 0.15 seconds if the enemy is knocked against a wall. * Rocket Punch can be empowered by ''Could not find 'Power Block' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'': ** An empowered Rocket Punch deals +50% damage, has +50% speed/distance, greatly increased blastback area, and the wall slam stuns the target for 0.15 - 0.6 seconds based on charge. * Rocket Punch has natural power limiters in the form of what is known as ‘Sliders’ & ‘Bouncers’. Both are intentional parts of controlling how often and easily punched targets will impact a surface and will only activate if specific conditions are met. ** A Slider will trigger if a punched target impacts a surface at an angle greater than 55° relative to their knockback trajectory. ** A Bouncer will trigger if a punched target impacts an obstacle in their trajectory path with less than 50% of their hitbox vertically. Bouncers tend to occur less often as they depend heavily on the shape of nearby geometry. * If Rocket Punch collides with another ability with Collide property ([[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]], [[File:Whirlwind Dash.png|sub|link=Vendetta#Whirlwind Dash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Vendetta#Whirlwind Dash|Whirlwind Dash]], a charging [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]], or another ''Could not find 'Rocket Punch' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'') both participants get [[Crowd Control#Knock Down|knocked down]] for 1.7 seconds. This is also called 'The Stalemate Mechanic'. ** The Blastback effect of Rocket Punch will ignore and override the Stalemate mechanic where applicable. * While the Melee keyword states that it is blocked by Ignore Melee abilities (e.g. [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]]), the Stun can go through and interrupt these abilities, which allows the punch to connect. ** If the target is [[Unstoppable]] or the Ignore Melee ability cannot be interrupted, for example Orisa during [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]], they will not take damage from the punch, but the blastback effect to enemies behind them will still apply. * Rocket Punch's wall-impact trajectory can be altered mid-motion by other knockback effects. * Rocket Punch can be cancelled by activating any other ability or pressing primary fire. ** If Rocket Punch is cancelled through aforementioned means by Doomfist during the charge, the empowered state is not consumed. But if the punch has already started, the empowered state will be consumed. * If Rocket Punch is interrupted by enemy crowd control, it goes on cooldown and the empowered state is lost. * Rocket Punch's speed buff while punching does not count towards global speed buff cap. * The punch is not stopped by fences or other breakables in its path, destroying them as it passes through.

* While charging, Rocket Punch can be cancelled by pressing the Primary Fire button.
* After being released, Rocket Punch can still be cancelled with the Jump key. This pushes Doomfist further than what he would have gone without cancelling.

raw.ability details: * Empowers Rocket Punch on landing. * While in Meteor Strike, Doomfist regenerates health and his ability cooldown rates are accelerated by 100%. * While in Meteor Strike, Doomfist is untargetable and invisible to by both allies and enemies and and cannot contest objectives while in Meteor Strike. He also becomes [[phased out]], cleansing most negative effects and stuck projectiles, such as [[Tracer]]'s [[File:Ability-tracer4.png|sub|link=Tracer#Pulse Bomb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Pulse Bomb|Pulse Bomb]]. * While targeting, the "Zoom Out" option on secondary fire allows moving to the highest elevation level as opposed to staying on current level. * Contrary to Meteor Strike’s display reticle, the Ultimate's area of effect is not a 2-D circle on the floor, but is actually a 3-D sphere. The display reticle is a cross section of the central point of the Ultimate. * If Doomfist selects his landing zone in a void/death pit, he will be instantly eliminated and will lose the Ultimate. * If there is a [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] at the landing location, Doomfist will slide to its side upon landing. * Meteor Strike's speed buff while targeting does not count towards global speed buff cap.

* This ability works even when used inside an interior area and will land in that area unless Doomfist moves outside.
* Holding right click enables a birds-eye view of the impact zone, which can also be used to climb up platforms.
* The impact zone is only visible to the enemy team once Doomfist is coming down.
* Doomfist is completely invincible during this ultimate.
* Meteor Strike has two rings that do different damage. If an enemy is touching both rings, the inner ring takes precedence.
* Doomfist's other abilities recharge twice as fast while Doomfist is in the air.

abilities.name: Flight

raw.shot type: [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Fly forward quickly and then free-fly briefly.
raw.ability details: * Reveals enemy health bars without having dealt damage to them. * Being [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[mobility lock]]ed prevent the ability from being activated and make Echo temporarily unable to fly, but do not completely end the ability. Only being [[hacked]] completely interrupts the ability. * Can be manually cancelled by pressing the ability key (default: LSHIFT) again. * Holding jump causes Echo to rise, while holding crouch causes her to descend. * When used, grants a speed burst of 28 m/s, which is sharply reduced to 8 m/s over ~0.5 seconds. * Vertical and horizontal speed are related. You can't have 8 meters per second of horizontal and vertical speed in same time. ** If the vertical speed is <0.25, then the horizontal maximum can be no more than 8 and vice versa. ** If your crosshair is exactly centered and you pinch forward/back and jump/crouch then your vertical and horizontal speed will be 5.66 meters per second. ** If your crosshair is exactly centered and you pinch forward/back and jump/crouch and right/left then your vertical speed = 4 and horizontal = 6.93. * The ability's maximum horizontal speed is 0.25 meters per second slower than the speed from the passive ability. * Movement acceleration in air increased by 100% when changing directions.

abilities.name: Duplicate

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted
raw.official description: Become a copy of the targeted hero. They cannot swap heroes for the duration.
raw.ability details: * Cannot target another Echo (even if that Echo is duplicating another hero) or non-playable characters, like [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] or [[Training Bots]]. * If Echo targets a D.Va pilot, Echo will still duplicate the mech. * Echo's HP instantly becomes the full base HP of her target, however this caps at 350 combined health. * Echo's target cannot switch heroes for the duration of the ability. * Echo gains access to her target's hero-specific [[Ping|Pings]] for the duration, such as those for a [[Sleep Dart|slept enemy]] or deployables such as [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]]. Most of these will not trigger a voice line. ** Echo cannot use these pings after Duplicate ends, even if the debuff or deployable is still active. * Duplicate does not gain any of the target's Perks. * Echo is [[phased out]] while transforming (cast recovery and reverting), becoming immune to damage and most negative [[status effect]]s. ** Sierra's [[File:Tracking Shot.png|sub|link=Sierra#Tracking Shot|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sierra#Tracking Shot|Tracking Shot]] will not be removed when transforming, and will continue to lock onto Echo after she finishes. * While Duplicate is active, Echo generates 5.5x ultimate charge from all sources. ** The patch notes are poorly worded with regard to the charge generated. Echo's ultimate generation is increased by an additional 450%, not multiplied by 4.5 times, for a total of 550%. * When the duration ends, or if Echo's HP is reduced to 0, Echo reverts back to normal. Her HP returns back to what it was before, or 50% of her max HP if it was lower than that. ** If Echo falls off the map while Duplicated, she will be instantly eliminated. * Reverting back to Echo interrupts whatever ability she was using. Most deployed abilities, buffs, and debuffs disappear after 3 seconds. ** Projectiles, turrets and damage or healing over time effects usually continue dealing damage or healing during this time, and in some cases will generate ultimate charge for Echo. ** [[Overhealth]] from Wrecking Ball's [[File:Adaptive_shield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Adaptive Shield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Adaptive Shield|Adaptive Shield]] and Lifeweaver's [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] persists even after 3 seconds have passed since Duplicate ended, and is instead removed as the ability normally would. ** Some projectiles, turrets, or damage over time effects fail to attribute their damage or kill credit to Echo after Duplicate has finished. This is also reflected on the kill feed, as while the ability and eliminated player will generally be correctly displayed, no player will be shown as responsible for the elimination. *** Affected abilities include: Junker Queen's [[File:Rampage_Icon.png|sub|link=Junker Queen#Rampage|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junker Queen#Rampage|Rampage]], Hazard's Kiriko's [[File:Kitsune_Rush_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Kitsune Rush|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Kitsune Rush|Kitsune Rush]] remains present but ceases buffing players. ** Baptiste's [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] and Mizuki's [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] remain present and functional, but will play their outro sound effects at the time they would have disappeared normally, even if they disappear early due to Duplicate ending. ** Anran's [[File:Passive Ignition.png|sub|link=Anran#Ignition|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Anran#Ignition|Ignition]] and Torbjörn's [[File:Ability-torbjorn5.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Molten Core|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Molten Core|Molten Core]] continue burning enemies, but do not deal any damage. ** Moira's [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Orb.png|sub|link=Moira#Biotic Orb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Orb|Biotic Orb]] ceases moving. ** Junkrat's [[File:Ability-Junkrat4.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Total Mayhem|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Total Mayhem|Total Mayhem]] will trigger if Duplicate ends due to HP being reduced to 0, but not if it ends due to maximum duration or falling off the map. * Echo can still use her own equipped voicelines and sprays while transformed, but not her or her target's emotes. * Echo will use the user's key bindings, options and crosshair for the targeted hero while transformed. * Echo copies the target's currently equipped skin. * In [[Overwatch Uprising]], Echo can target a [[Null Sector]] [[OR14]] or a [[Bastion Siege Automaton B73]], duplicating a standard [[Orisa]] or [[Bastion]] respectively.

abilities.name: Deflect

raw.official description: Deflect incoming projectiles towards the direction you are aiming and block melee attacks.
raw.duration:
0.27 seconds min. duration
2 seconds max. duration
0.192 seconds recovery
raw.ability details: * Changing the direction of the character's gaze (and hence the reflection zone) takes time. It takes ~0.14 seconds to be able to deflect something that was exactly behind you. * Can be cancelled manually by reactivating the ability or activating wall climb, Swift Strike or Dragonblade. ** If you cancel Deflect with wall climb, then the recovery will not be activated. * The hitbox extends to cover Genji's sides, but only attacks coming from less than 90° of the direction Genji is facing are affected. * Reflected projectiles copy most of the properties of the original attack. ** Any projectile reflected is treated as Genji's, and its damage falloff is reset. ** Most abilities that deal splash damage can also deal self-damage, meaning that Genji can damage himself with them. ** Healing projectiles can also be reflected to heal your allies. ** Homing or guided projectiles lose this property when reflected and travel straight forward in Genji's aiming direction. * Blocks [[melee]] and extended melee attacks, such as [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]] and [[File:Whip Shot.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Whip Shot|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Whip Shot|Whip Shot]]. These attacks are not reflected when blocked. ** Doomfist's Rocket Punch, despite being classified as Melee cannot be blocked, due to its stun interrupting the channel. * Cannot block [[beam]] and AoE attacks, collision/movement-based attacks, or attacks marked as Strong Projectiles, such as [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|sub|link=Sigma#Accretion|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Accretion|Accretion]].

duration.raw:
0.27 seconds min. duration
2 seconds max. duration
0.192 seconds recovery

* Destroys fences and other breakables when mantling a ledge.

abilities.name: Jagged Wall

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile (initial)
raw.official description: Launch a spiked wall that damages and knocks back nearby enemies.
raw.ability details: * Cooldown begins as soon as it's fired. * The projectile will transform into a wall upon impacting the ground, ceiling, wall or a surface of another terrain-like object. ** The projectile doesn't collide with enemy [[heroes]]. ** Projectile can be blocked by [[barriers]] while in flight, but contact with the deployed wall cannot. ** Projectile can be destroyed by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] while in flight, but contact with the deployed wall cannot be blocked. ** If the object the wall is attached to is destroyed, Jagged Wall will be destroyed as well. ** The deployed wall can be manually destroyed by pressing the ability button again. * Enemies that touch the wall take damage and are [[knocked back]]. Whenever an enemy is hit by the wall, there is a 1.75 second delay before they can be hit by it again. * Deployed Jagged Wall is a terrain-like [[object]]; it has collision and blocks line of sight. ** On ground and ceiling surfaces, the wall will be oriented longer side facing the player. When deployed on wall surfaces, the top side will be facing away from the wall and the shorter side will be facing up as the new top surface. ** The wall has a walkable top surface regardless of the orientation it was deployed in. * Hazard can use Vault on the wall, and touch a friendly wall without taking damage. * Wall cannot be damaged or destroyed by [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]]. * Wall can be lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] if attached to it. * Unlike most other objects, Jagged Wall does not get crushed by [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]]. The tree can be deployed on top of Jagged Wall or next to it but not in the same space. * Jagged Wall may prevent pillars from [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] from rising if the latter is attempted to be deployed in the same space.

* Can reach up to 5.7 meters from ground (6.5m with jump) due to momentum being kept.
* The obstacle must be at least 1.25 meters tall to trigger Vault.
* Hazard destroys fences and other breakables when climbing near them.

abilities.name: Spike Guard

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile
raw.official description: Protect yourself from frontal damage. Firing homing spikes at nearby enemies and regenerating ammo.
raw.fire rate: 6 shots/s
raw.ability details: * Damage per second: 70 * Resource meter recharges over 4.29 seconds. * Has a 0.75 second regen delay. * Can fire up to 18 shots per target over the full duration. * Spike Guard is a [[channeled]] ability; Hazard cannot use other abilities or fire while it is active and it is interrupted if he is stunned, knocked down or hacked. ** The impact damage from the instance that applied an effect that interrupted Spike Guard will still be blocked. ** Activating any other ability deactivates Spike Guard. If secondary fire is held, Spike Guard starts again when the previous ability finishes. * While active, periodically fires 1 missile at each enemy within the area, dealing damage to first enemy in their way. ** Targets heroes and all [[objects]], excluding [[barriers]]. ** Enemies can be targeted through barriers, but barriers still will block projectiles trying to pass through them. ** Ammo only regenerates while dealing damage to an enemy, including objects. ** Piercing and Area of Effect keywords are misleading. Only the targeting has an "area of effect", and the produced projectiles do not pierce heroes, but can be blocked by other heroes on their way to the intended target. * Regenerates 1 ammo every 0.44 seconds, as long as you deal continuous damage to the target(s) for the 0.44s. * Damage is blocked if the horizontal angle between the direction Hazard is facing and the direction from the impact location to its source is less than 90 degrees. ** Hazard's vertical view angle does not affect blocking or targeting. ** Effectiveness extends to area abilities and splash damage from projectiles. Damage is generally blocked when facing the center of the AoE. *** Damage from [[File:Ability-hanzo4.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Dragonstrike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Dragonstrike|Dragonstrike]] and [[File:Ability-torbjorn5.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Molten Core|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Molten Core|Molten Core]] is blocked regardless of facing direction. *** Does not reduce damage from [[Sombra]]'s [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] or Sigma's [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] (incl. both lift and slam). ** Spike Guard mitigates delayed or triggered detonation damage from sticky projectiles and debuffs, regardless of the direction Hazard is facing. *** Following abilities are affected: [[File:A-36_Icon.png|sub|link=Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|A-36 Tactical Grenade]], [[File:STICKY BOMBS.png|sub|link=Echo#Sticky Bombs|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Echo#Sticky Bombs|Sticky Bombs]], [[File:Ability-tracer4.png|sub|link=Tracer#Pulse Bomb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Pulse Bomb|Pulse Bomb]] and [[File:Captive_sun_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Captive Sun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Captive Sun|Captive Sun]]. ** Does not affect [[damage over time]] effects from abilities.

raw.official description: Rain down a torrent of spikes, Immobilizing enemies.
raw.ability details: * Downpour's cast can be interrupted from being hit by [[crowd control]] during its startup. * Hazard loses 50% of his aerial momentum while casting Downpour. * A hitscan attack is fired from above each enemy in the area at an angle of 70°. ** Targets can be hit by shots aimed at targets under them, causing them to block it and take the damage instead. ** The area and spikes are not blocked by terrain. ** The spikes can be blocked by [[barrier]]s, destroyed by D.Va's [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], Orisa's [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], Sigma's [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], Doomfist's [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]], and Mizuki's [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by Genji's [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] on the way down. ** The initial animation, where Hazard fires the shots upwards, is only a visual effect and does not produce projectiles that can be interacted with in any way. ** Also targets [[objects]], excluding [[barriers]]. * Spikes start falling in front of Hazard 0.9 seconds after the startup finishes. The rain then extends away from Hazard, reaching the maximum range over 0.7 seconds. * Enemies hit by Downpour take damage and are [[rooted]] for 2.8 seconds.

raw.fire rate: 1.25 shots/s
raw.ability details: * Heal per second: 50 (allies), 25 (self) * Healing prioritizes the hero with the lowest health percentage within the pylon's range. * Cooldown starts immediately upon activation. * If a new Pylon is deployed while one is still active, the old one will be destroyed. * A deployed Pylon can be destroyed using the Interact key at any time. If it hasn't taken damage within the last 3 seconds, this has no effect on its cooldown. * If the pylon is damaged by the enemy, a new pylon cannot be deployed for 3 seconds. If the pylon is destroyed during this time, either by the enemy or manually by the player, Healing Pylon goes on a longer cooldown, starting from the moment it was destroyed. * Pylon can attach itself to terrain and terrain-like [[objects]], like [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. ** Bounces off of [[barrier]]s, other [[deployables]] and fences and other breakables. ** Pylon can attach to [[payload]], but not to the [[TS-1]] robot or the barricade in [[Push]]. *** Pylon is destroyed if it is in the way of a moving payload or barricade (but not the robot). ** Healing Pylon can be placed on [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] and lifted by it regardless of which was placed first. ** Pylon is destroyed if a [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] is deployed on top of it. ** Deploying an [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or using [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]] on the pylon covers it, blocking line of sight and preventing it from healing. ** If the pylon touches [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or stays within [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]], it will "freeze" and stop healing until those abilities end. ** If the object it was attached to expires or is destroyed the pylon falls on ground. * The pylon has collision with heroes and blocks shots, but does not block line of sight for AoE. * [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] will destroy the pylon if he collides with it. This will stop the charge. * Stunning movement abilities, like [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] or [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]] (during [[File:Rally.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Rally|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Rally|Rally]]), can "stun" the pylon, briefly stopping its healing. * Healing Pylon is unaffected by [[knockback]]. * Pylon cannot be targeted by [[File:icon-ability.tacticalvisor.png|sub|link=Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|Tactical Visor]], [[File:Ability-mccree4.png|sub|link=Cassidy#Deadeye|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Cassidy#Deadeye|Deadeye]] or [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Orb.png|sub|link=Moira#Biotic Orb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Orb|Biotic Orb]]. * Pylon can be targeted by [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]], [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turret]] and [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]. * A deployed pylon can be teleported using Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]]. * Sombra can [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]] the pylon, disabling it for 10 seconds. * The pylon projectile before deployment cannot be blocked by [[barrier]]s, [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] or reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], however the healing projectiles shot by it can. * If the pylon breaks, a "Destroyed" UI will display for Illari. * The interact key is shown to be blocked by a red cross while Illari is dead, but this seems to be a mistake as the key can still be pressed to break the pylon. * If the pylon breaks while Illari is dead and is thus put on cooldown, due to an enemy attack or from Illari pressing the interact key within 3 seconds of the pylon taking damage, the otherwise 14-second cooldown of the pylon will immediately reset upon Illari respawning. * If Illari is instead revived with [[File:Ability-mercy5.png|sub|link=Mercy#Resurrect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mercy#Resurrect|Resurrect]] by [[Mercy]], the 14-second cooldown will ''not'' reset.

raw.official description: Fire an explosive ball of solar energy. Enemies hit are slowed and explode after taking significant damage.
raw.cast time:
0 + 1.2 seconds (launch)
0.02 + 0.6 seconds (solar energy ball)
raw.radius:
10 meters (impact)
1 - 5 meters (Sunstruck explosion, falloff start - max)
raw.ability details: * Captive Sun is a [[channeled]] ability; it is interrupted by becoming [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], including subsidiary effects, consuming the ultimate charge. * Launches Illari into the air granting her free flight for the duration and her next shot is replaced by solar energy ball. ** [[Rooted]] and [[hindered]] make Illari temporarily unable to fly, but do not prevent the activation or immediately end the ability. ** Upon launching, fully restores the energy of [[File:Solar_rifle_alt_icon.png|middle|link=Illari#Solar Rifle Alt Fire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Solar Rifle Alt Fire|Solar Rifle Alt Fire]] and removes the overheated status. ** After the launch, the player has 4 seconds to fire the solar energy ball. *** The ball is automatically fired at the end of the timer. ** Projectile passes through enemies (but not shields) and explodes only upon contact with a shield, terrain or terrain-like [[object]], like [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], or if it has travelled for 10 seconds. ** Any enemy heroes, including [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]], damaged by the direct hit or splash of the solar energy ball are marked and [[slowed]] for 6 seconds. *** Slow slowly decays over the duration. * If a marked enemy receives total of 90 damage after reductions or is killed, they will become "SUNSTRUCK", causing the mark to explode after 0.8 seconds dealing damage in an area. ** Damage from the direct hit and splash from Captive Sun does not count toward this, but the damage from another player's Sunstruck explosion does. ** Only one mark can be active at a time. For example, if an allied Echo duplicates Illari and hits the same target with Captive Sun. *** If the target is hit with another Captive Sun before Sunstruck is triggered, the duration of the mark is refreshed. *** If the target is hit with another Captive Sun during Sunstruck explosion delay, a new mark will not be applied. ** The mark can be removed by Zarya's [[File:Ability-zarya2.png|sub|link=Zarya#Particle Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Particle Barrier|Particle/Projected Barrier]], Zenyatta's [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]], D.Va's [[File:Ability-dva6.png|sub|link=D.Va#Call Mech|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Call Mech|Call Mech]] or by becoming [[phased out]], such as through [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]] or [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]]. *** However, if the target has already been Sunstruck, the explosion will happen regardless. Damage may still be prevented. *** [[Kiriko]] and [[Sombra]] can teleport, protecting themselves from the explosion. However, they carry the explosion with them, harming anyone around their destination. If a Sunstruck ally happens to be there, they could explode. * Sunstruck explosion has damage falloff, reducing damage by up to 25%. * The projectile can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but the splash and Sunstruck explosion are unaffected. * Direct damage and splash damage from Captive Sun are affected by [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], as long as the projectile passes through it. However, the Sunstruck explosion damage is not. * The projectile's environment collision size is 0 meters, so to collide with the environment the projectile's center would have to directly hit it. * Captive Sun's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

raw.official description: Frenetic Flight can knockback an enemy on impact when flying fast enough, dealing 50 damage on impact.
Permanent flight.
raw.ability details: * Moving forward goes in the exact direction Jetpack Cat faces, so looking up makes her fly up and looking down makes her fly down. ** Conversely, moving backwards while looking up makes her fly downwards and looking down while moving backwards makes her fly upwards. * Additionally, holding jump makes Jetpack Cat fly upwards and holding crouch makes her fly downwards. * Not affected by [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]].'"`UNIQ--ref-00000042-QINU`"' * Falls to the ground when hindered, knocked down, or frozen, or abilities that cause falling (e.g. [[File:Downpour.png|sub|link=Hazard#Downpour|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Downpour|Downpour]], [[File:Ability Binding Chain.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Binding Chain|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Binding Chain|Binding Chain]], [[File:Palantine_fang.png|middle|link=Vendetta#Palatine Fang|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Vendetta#Palatine Fang|Palatine Fang]]'s overhead strike).'"`UNIQ--ref-00000043-QINU`"' * Jetpack Cat cannot use jump pads nor activate elevators. ** However, anything that causes Jetpack Cat to fall to the ground (such as [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]]) will allow her to activate jump pads and elevators.'"`UNIQ--ref-00000044-QINU`"'

raw.official description: Toggle into transport mode, allowing an ally to be towed. Increases movement speed and heals your ally.
raw.heal: 25 per second (ally)
raw.ability details: * Unlike [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]], allies may choose to interact to be carried by Lifeline. ** The text "Grappled" will appear on the ally's screen while being carried. ** B.O.B. will automatically be carried if in the selection radius. ** If D.Va activates [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]] while being lifelined, the self-destructing mech (not the pilot) will continue to be carried. '"`UNIQ--ref-0000004E-QINU`"' * For the ally attached to Lifeline, using movement abilities or pressing space will disconnect the tether. ** When disconnected, momentum is carried, allowing for additional movement after use. * For Jetpack Cat, using primary fire, her ultimate, or pressing the key/button again will end Lifeline. ** However, she can use [[File:Ability Frenetic Flight.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|Frenetic Flight]] and ''Could not find 'Purr' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' while using Lifeline. * Cannot carry connected targets ''through'' terrain, disconnecting them if they get too far from Jetpack Cat. * Abilities that require the player to be on the ground to use (Such as [[File:Ability-Junkrat5.png|sub|link=Junkrat#RIP-Tire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#RIP-Tire|RIP-Tire]] or [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]]) cannot be used while being carried in the air by Lifeline. ** They can still be used if tethered while on the ground.

raw.official description: Dive towards a ground location, knocking down enemies and tethering the nearest one to you.
raw.heal: 30 healing per second (self)
raw.ability details: * Fully recharges [[File:Ability Frenetic Flight.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|Frenetic Flight]] energy meter on use. * The ultimate can only be used if targeting a flat surface or slope. ** If used on a wall, it will automatically target the floor below the wall, if in sight. ** Will automatically target the floor at the max range if pointing beyond the range. ** Cannot be used if targeting areas outside the environment, and will instead target the closest surface from that area. * Jetpack Cat automatically activates [[File:Ability Lifeline.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|Lifeline]] when the ultimate is used: if no enemies are hit, Lifeline will remain active until disabled. * Jetpack Cat can use [[File:Ability Purr.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Purr|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Purr|Purr]], [[File:Ability Frenetic Flight.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Frenetic Flight|Frenetic Flight]] and [[quick melee]] while carrying an enemy, but not their primary fire. * Unlike with [[File:Ability Lifeline.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|Lifeline]], connected enemies cannot exit the tether early with movement abilities or jumping. ** Only a very few abilities can break the tether. A few examples are: [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]], [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]], [[File:Overrun_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Overrun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Overrun|Overrun]], and an enemy cat's ''Could not find 'Catnapper' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.''. ** Abilities that you might intuitively expect to break the tether do not (such as [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]]). ** However, abilities that dont break tether but do hinder JPC (such as Flashbang) are considered counters. * Can be cancelled by Jetpack Cat by pressing Ultimate again or by activating Lifeline. ** Unlike Lifeline, cannot be cancelled by Jetpack Cat pressing primary fire. * Connected targets can get stuck on terrain, disconnecting them if they get too far from Jetpack Cat. * If an enemy D.Va has been catnapped, and then activates [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], the mech (not the pilot) will remain tethered. ** However, a mech that is already self-destructing cannot be catnapped. * Using Catnapper to drop an enemy off a cliff grants zero ult charge. '"`UNIQ--ref-00000055-QINU`"''"`UNIQ--ref-00000056-QINU`"' ** However, hitting them with [[File:Ability Purr.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Purr|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Purr|Purr]] after dropping them will change it to a Purr environmental kill, granting ult charge. '"`UNIQ--ref-00000057-QINU`"'

raw.official description: Heal from all damage over time dealt by wounds.
raw.heal: 250% of wound damage dealt
raw.ability details:
* Self-healing is not reduced by the global healing reduction passive.
* Adrenaline Rush instantly heals for the remaining wound damage when an enemy dies with wounds on them.
* Healing received from Adrenaline Rush grants ultimate charge.
* Wound damage - and therefore also healing - can be affected by damage amplifications on Junker Queen and reductions on the target.
* Wound is a [[damage over time]] effect, it can be Cleansed and its damage ignores damage reduction from [[armor]].
** Adrenaline Rush healing is tied to the wound effect, resulting in no healing received if the wound is removed.
* Each enemy can have one wound per ability. Reapplying a wound with the same ability refreshes its duration.
** The wounds from return impact and quick melee of Jagged Blade are considered the same ability, but the wound from the direct impact is considered separate.

heal.raw: 250% of wound damage dealt

Only effects that completely free the target from the trap can remove it: ** This includes [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]], [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]], [[File:Ability Sombra Translocator.png|sub|link=Sombra#Translocator|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Translocator|Translocator]] (pre-thrown), being lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] or [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] and teleporting with [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]]. ** [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]], [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]] and [[File:Ability-zarya2.png|sub|link=Zarya#Particle Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Particle Barrier|Particle/Projected Barrier]] - which normally could cleanse anti-heal and be used while trapped - cannot remove the anti-heal from Aluminum Frame due to not being able to remove the "Trapped" status.

raw.official description: Deals no damage to self with explosives. Drop bombs on death.
raw.ability details: * The grenades will be dropped at wherever Junkrat was killed, and do not inherit his momentum. Being killed while flying through the air will drop the grenades straight down to the ground. * The grenades can be negated by [[D.Va]]'s [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[Orisa]]'s [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[Sigma]]'s [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[Doomfist]]'s [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[Mizuki]]'s [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and they can be deflected by [[Genji]]'s [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] before exploding. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. Splash damage alone going through is not amplified. * The grenades can penetrate [[barrier]]s, but their explosions cannot.
