raw.official description: Throw a knockback mine with LSHIFT then detonate it with [[File:Key mouse 2.png|20x20px|link=]].
raw.cast time: 0.128 second for throw + 0.112 s. recovery for detonation
raw.ability details: * The mine sticks to any terrain and bounces off of players and [[objects]] other than [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. * The mine bounces once upon hitting a [[barrier]] for the first time, but can then pass through it. The explosion is blocked by barriers. * The mine can be destroyed by enemy players, causing no explosion. * The projectile can be destroyed by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]],[[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] before it attaches to a surface. The explosion is unaffected. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. Explosion damage alone going through is not amplified. * Upon cast time ends, you can change the future flight path. * If the ability is interrupted before the mine begins to exist, the cooldown will not start and the charge will not be spent. * If Junkrat throws another mine while one is already deployed the first mine will instantly and silently disappear. * The mine does not deal self-damage, but still applies the knockback, making it usable as a mobility tool. * Junkrat and enemies caught in the blast are knocked up 45° vertically, regardless of their position relative to the mine. * Horizontal knockback is away from the center of the mine. *If Junkrat is hacked, the remote will be disabled.

raw.official description: Place an immobilizing trap.
raw.cast time:
instantly + 0.33 s recovery
.75 second arming time
raw.ability details: * Similarly to his [[File:Ability-Junkrat2.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Concussion Mine|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Concussion Mine|Concussion Mine]], deploying a second Steel Trap will remove the first. * The trap sticks to ground and [[objects]] with a walkable surface, and bounces off of players, walls and other object surfaces. * The trap bounces once upon hitting a [[barrier]] for the first time, but can then fall through it. * An unactivated trap can be destroyed by enemy players. * Junkrat can see the trap's location through walls. * The player receives a cue when an enemy triggers or destroys the trap. * Being in phased out state or affected by Zarya's [[File:Ability-zarya2.png|sub|link=Zarya#Particle Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Particle Barrier|Particle/Protective Barrier]] will prevent a trap from triggering, but will not free a player that is already trapped. * Orisa can break free from the trap by activating [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]]. * Lifeweaver can pull the target free with [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]. * Sombra can escape from the trap with [[File:Ability Sombra Translocator.png|sub|link=Sombra#Translocator|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Translocator|Translocator]], however it must be in air when the trap activates, as it is a movement ability that gets disabled by the trap. * If a trapped player is moved sufficiently far from it, e.g. by using Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]] or being lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] or [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], the trap will break and the target will be freed. However, if the trap was placed on the object, the player will remain trapped even if lifted, as the trap can move with it.

The nade then freezes in place for 0,3 second pre-detonation, during which the explosion can't be stopped by any means. If the nade is shot bouncing on the ground, or just bounce off a wall/an object, it'll explode if it bounces a second time less than 1,5 second after the first bounce. Nades can bounce on walls and objects several times without exploding. * The projectile size is decreased after its second bounce. * The velocity of the projectile is slightly reduced after each bounce. * The projectile can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but the splash is unaffected. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. raw.official description: Glide horizontally with increased movement speed.
raw.ability details:
* Cooldown starts when the duration ends.
* Grants a short dash at the start, followed by a longer movement speed boost.
* Juno is unaffected by gravity while Glide Boost is active.
* Juno is airborne while Glide Boost is active. As a result, she cannot crouch while gliding, and jumping will count as the double jump from Martian Overboots, meaning that she can only jump once if used while on ground.
* Glide Boost does not have a vertical component, however the player can still gain or reduce altitude by gliding against slanted or vertical terrain.
* To gain altitude against a wall or other vertical terrain, stand up against the wall, face towards it or away from it, then glide sideways, parallel to the wall, in the direction you want to move.
* Movement acceleration in air is reduced when changing directions, after the initial movement burst.
* Can be aborted early by pressing the ability button again.
* Glide Boost's initial dash [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap, but the lingering speed buff does.

raw.ability details: * Cooldown starts when the projectiles are fired. * Cancelling Pulsar Torpedos before locking onto a target incurs a 2 second cooldown instead of the usual 10. * When activated, Pulsar Torpedoes starts to acquire targets, and when reactivated fires homing projectiles at once towards the targets it has locked onto. ** Only targets heroes, incl. [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]. ** Cannot be fired while no heroes have yet to be completely locked on to. *** Pulsar Torpedoes goes on 2 second cooldown if it ends or is interrupted without ever locking onto a target. ** Fires automatically when targeting duration ends if any targets are locked on. ** Time required to lock on scales based on distance to the target, starting from 0.5 seconds at 5 meters to 1 second at maximum targeting range. ** Targets must be visible and within Juno's line of sight to progress the lock on and for the missiles to be fired at them. *** If a target temporarily leaves Juno's line of sight or moves more than 5 meters beyond its targeting range, lock on progress will be paused and continues from the previous state when they reappear or enter the targeting range again. *** Projectiles cannot target or be fired at enemies or allies using any of the following abilities: [[File:Ability Doomfist Meteor Strike.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Meteor Strike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Meteor Strike|Meteor Strike]], [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]], [[File:Ability-tracer3.png|sub|link=Tracer#Recall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Recall|Recall]] and [[File:Burrow_icon.png|sub|link=Venture#Burrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Venture#Burrow|Burrow]]. *** Damage projectiles can lock onto and fired at heroes that are [[phased out]] or [[invincible]]. ** Once fired, damage projectiles lose homing if the target is/becomes phased out, invincible, excluding [[Zarya]]'s barriers, or invisible, however the projectile may start homing again when the target reappears. ** [[D.Va]] ejected from her mech and [[Echo]] duplicating a hero (or transforming back) are considered separate targets from the original. These events reset the lock on while targeting, and cause already fired projectiles to lose homing. * Pulsar Torpedoes is a [[channeled]] ability; Juno cannot use other abilities or fire while it is active and it is interrupted if Juno is [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]]. ** Pulsar Torpedoes can be manually cancelled by pressing secondary fire again. ** ''Could not find 'Glide Boost' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' and [[File:Ability-Juno1.png|sub|link=Juno#Martian Overboots|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Juno#Martian Overboots|Martian Overboots]] can be used while channeling without interrupting the channel. ** Activating ''Could not find 'Orbital Ray' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' interrupts the channel. * Projectiles do not have maximum range once they are fired, only targeting range is limited. * Damage projectiles will collide with the first enemy hero or object in their way, whereas healing projectiles only hit their designated targets. ** Enemy hero may take more than one damage projectiles damage. * [[Barrier]]s will block projectiles, but do not prevent targeting. * If the target moves beyond terrain, the healing projectiles can follow allies through it, but damage projectiles will be blocked by it. * Projectiles reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] do not home.

raw.official description: Jump at walls to climb up them.
raw.ability details:
* Wall Climb ends when the player stops holding the button, the wall ends or the duration is up. It cannot then be used again until the player has landed on a walkable surface.
* Kiriko will grab and mantle ledges if the input is held while facing a ledge of a walkable surface.
** This does not count as starting Wall Climb, and can be done even if Wall Climb was already used.
** Destroys fences and other breakables in the way when mantling a ledge.
* Can reach up to 10.3m height (11m with jump) due to momentum being kept after wall climb ends.

raw.official description: Channel a burst of healing talismans that can seek targeted allies.

0.2 seconds (per burst)
1.42 seconds (full clip)
raw.reload time: 0.9 seconds
raw.ability details: * Healing per second: 182 while firing (80.53 w/ reload) * Default behavior is a toggle, meaning pressing the button will cause Kiriko to hold down the ability. If you don't want to use all 10 Healing Ofuda, you must press again to toggle off. This setting can be changed in the options. * After being thrown, the talismans are either yellow or blue. The color indicates whether they are homing in on a target. ** Yellow means they are seeking the allied player. ** Blue means they don't have a target. Blue ofuda can still heal an ally if they walk into the missed ofuda's path. * Healing Ofuda are destroyed upon impact with terrain (whether they were homing or not). * Healing Ofuda automatically reload when they are not in use or the clip is emptied. All 10 Ofuda are restored, regardless of how many Ofuda were left in the clip. * The max range for Healing Ofuda is the same as [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]], so you can use the indicator for that ability to determine if your Healing Ofuda is within range of homing in or not. * The maximum view angle for Healing Ofuda lock on is half that of [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]]. If [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]] sensitivity is set to 50%, then the presence of the [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]] reticle will also indicate an adequate viewing angle for Healing Ofuda lock-on. * Healing Ofuda will continue to home on targets even when they teleport or become untargetable by enemies, such as a [[File:Ability-reaper3.png|sub|link=Reaper#Shadow Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Shadow Step|Shadow Stepping]] Reaper or a Moira using [[File:Ability Moira Fade.png|sub|link=Moira#Fade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Fade|Fade]]. They are still limited by their max homing range. * Despite [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] switching ownership to Genji, yellow ofudas will still try to seek their original target. If Genji's allies stand in their path, they will be healed. * Can destroy fences and other breakables when colliding with them.

raw.official description: Summon a fox spirit that rushes forward, accelerating the movement, attack speed, and cooldowns of allies that follow its path.
raw.ability details: * Grants +30% move speed, +50% reload and attack speed, and +100% cooldown recharge rate. ** The bonuses persist for 1 second after leaving the area. * Kitsune Rush's deployment is completely stopped if the fox spirit collides with terrain under certain conditions. Whatever path was laid prior to collision with terrain remains in effect. ** If the fox hits a surface at a low angle, it will slide along it, maintaining its original direction, instead of stopping. ** The fox can jump walls roughly up to Kiriko's height, but it will not cover gaps. If Kitsune Rush goes down a ledge, it will deploy along the ground beneath regardless of height. ** Destroys fences and other breakables in its path. * Kitsune Rush's deployment is not impacted by barriers and will pass through them. ** Terrain-like [[objects]], such as Mei's [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] and [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]] and Lifeweaver's [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]], can block the deployment of Kitsune's Rush, regardless of whether the ability is from an ally or an enemy. * [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B]] receives the benefits of Kitsune Rush. ** Other [[deployables]], like Torbjorn's [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Turret]] and Junkrat's [[File:Ability-Junkrat5.png|sub|link=Junkrat#RIP-Tire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#RIP-Tire|RIP-Tire]] do not receive any benefit. * Functions with [[File:Ability_Moira_Coalescence.png|sub|40x40px|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Beam|Beam ]] Beam-type weapons, such as [[File:Ability-winston1.png|middle|link=Winston#Tesla Cannon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Tesla Cannon|Tesla Cannon]], by increasing the tick rate. * Works on abilities which have a rate of fire, such as [[File:Ability-hanzo03.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Storm Arrows|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Storm Arrows|Storm Arrows]] and [[File:Ability-Roadhog4.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Whole Hog|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Whole Hog|Whole Hog]]. * Speeds up the charge time of most weapons, such as [[File:Ability-hanzo1.png|middle|link=Hanzo#Storm Bow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Storm Bow|Storm Bow]] and [[File:Healing_Blossom_Icon.png|middle|link=Lifeweaver#Healing Blossom|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Healing Blossom|Healing Blossom]]. * Kitsune Rush cannot be cancelled mid-animation by [[Crowd Control|stuns]], but getting killed or hacked mid-animation does. * Kitsune Rush's [[movement speed]] buff counts towards global movement speed buff cap.

* Landing 16 thorns deals 96 base damage, for a total of 136 after detonation.
* Increases Thorn Volley's max DPS from 131.868 to 184.615 (not counting reloads).
* [[Deployables]] and [[Barriers]] cannot be affected .

raw.shot type: [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile
raw.official description: Throw a platform that springs upwards when stepped on. Resets when not in use.
raw.ability details: * Cooldown starts when the platform is thrown. * The projectile bounces off of barriers and other [[objects]] and only deploys upon hitting the ground. * The platform is a terrain-like [[object]], which has collision and blocks line of sight. ** [[File:Grappling_claw.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Grappling Claw|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Grappling Claw|Grappling Claw]] and [[File:Ability-widowmaker2.png|sub|link=Widowmaker#Grappling Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Widowmaker#Grappling Hook|Grappling Hook]] can attach to the platform. * [[Payload]] and [[Push]] [[TS-1|robot]] will phase through the platform if it was placed in their path. ** It cannot be activated by them, nor activated whilst underneath them. * The platform lasts indefinitely, until activated or destroyed, while on ground. The duration counts down only while the platform is raised. * The platform is activated when an allied or enemy hero (including [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]) steps on it. If no one is standing on the platform, it will descend after 2 seconds. ** The platform can lift resurrectable souls, but they will not activate the lift themselves. ** The activation has 2 meter radius upon impact, reduced to 0.75 meters after initial placement. ** The activation has a brief delay, which allows it not to be activated when jumping on it or passing over with fast movement abilities, unless the platform was deployed directly under a hero. ** The platform may rise up to 9 meters height from the surface it was deployed on. ** If the platform's center is below the payload/push bot, terrain or terrain-like objects, it will not rise even if a hero stands on it. *** The activated platform may also stop without reaching maximum height if there is not enough vertical clearance. * Lifeweaver can destroy the platform by using the ability 1 key or by deploying a new one. ** There is an option in controls to use the interact key to destroy the platform instead of the ability 1 key. *** Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]] has the priority if interact is used in its vicinity. * Can affect enemies that are immune to [[Crowd Control]], like Orisa during [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] or Mei during [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]]. * The momentum from the lift allows heroes to jump higher than normal when reaching the top. * Abilities and objects can be placed on the platform, and in most cases moved by the platform: ** Torbjörn's [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Turret]], Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]], Winston's [[File:Ability-winston3.png|sub|link=Winston#Barrier Projector|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Barrier Projector|Barrier Projector]], Illari's [[File:Healing_pylon_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Healing Pylon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Healing Pylon|Healing Pylon]] and souls that Mercy can [[File:Ability-mercy5.png|sub|link=Mercy#Resurrect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mercy#Resurrect|Resurrect]] can be moved by the platform, regardless of which order they were placed. ** ''Could not find 'Tree of Life' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'', [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turrets]], [[File:Ability-Junkrat2.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Concussion Mine|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Concussion Mine|Concussion Mine]], [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]], [[File:icon-ability.bioticfield.png|sub|link=Soldier: 76#Biotic Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Soldier: 76#Biotic Field|Biotic Field]], [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]] and Mauga's [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]] can only be moved by the platform if they were placed on the platform afterwards. ** [[File:Baptiste-ability3.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Immortality Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Immortality Field|Immortality Field]] and [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]] activate upon landing on the platform, but will not move with it. ** [[File:Minefield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Minefield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Minefield|Minefield]] mines can be lifted by the platform before they are primed. Lifted mines remain mid-air or explode due to contact. * Most entities placed on a platform will fall down if it is destroyed. ** If [[Mei]]'s [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[Ramattra]]'s [[File:Void_Barrier_Icon.png|sub|link=Ramattra#Void Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ramattra#Void Barrier|Void Barrier]], [[Baptiste]]'s [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], or [[Wrecking Ball]]'s [[File:Minefield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Minefield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Minefield|Minefield]] mines are placed on the platform and said platform is destroyed, they will remain suspended in midair. ** [[Hazard]]'s [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]] is destroyed if it is attached to a platform that is destroyed. * The platform can be [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hacked]], preventing it from activating while on ground for 10 seconds or destroying an already active platform. Hacking Lifeweaver does not disable the platform. * Can lift players out of [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], provided it can be activated. * The platform is unaffected by Ramattra's [[File:Ravenous_Vortex_Icon.png|sub|link=Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|Ravenous Vortex]]. * Heroes affected by [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]] can be lifted by the platform, breaking the trap in the process if the trap was placed on the ground first. * Venture will automatically move onto the platform (while it is on the ground) in [[File:Burrow_icon.png|sub|link=Venture#Burrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Venture#Burrow|Burrow]], but cannot activate it. ** Breaking the petal while venture is burrowed in it and emerge-charging will force-cancel their burrow.'"`UNIQ--ref-0000008E-QINU`"''"`UNIQ--ref-0000008F-QINU`"' *** This can be used as a technique: quickly place and cancel petal above a burrowed emerge-charging Venture. * The projectile bounces off of fences and other breakables (including ones with a walkable surface), but breaks any that collide with the platform as it deploys.

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted (ally)
raw.official description: Pull an ally to your location, protecting them as they travel.
raw.heal: 75 (ally)
raw.ability details: * Only usable on heroes, including [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]. * The targeted player is pulled to the location, where the ability was activated from, as opposed to Lifeweaver's current location. * Prevents the target from taking [[Crowd Control]] and damage during the pull, but does not cleanse debuffs applied beforehand. ** Can pull allies out of Immobilizing abilities, such as: [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]], [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]], [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]], [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], etc. ** Projectiles that hit the target during the pull are blocked, as opposed to passing through, but it does not block line of sight for AoE attacks. ** Players will momentarily stop taking damage from [[damage over time]] effects, such as [[Adrenaline Rush|wounds]] inflicted by [[Junker Queen]], while being pulled. In Junker Queen's case, it also decreases her self-healing, as it is based on damage dealt. * Life Grip can pull allies channeling an ability but it will not interrupt the channel. * After 0.75 seconds, the target can use [[Movement speed#Movement abilities|movement abilities]], such as [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], during the pull to get out of it. ** Life Grip will, however, interrupt any on-going movement abilities when it is activated. *** This doesn't apply to Mauga's [[File:Overrun_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Overrun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Overrun|Overrun]] however, which will pause during the pull and continue once it ends. * Life Grip cannot be used on allies casting one of the following abilities: [[File:Ability-dva6.png|sub|link=D.Va#Call Mech|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Call Mech|Call Mech]], [[File:Ability Doomfist Meteor Strike.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Meteor Strike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Meteor Strike|Meteor Strike]], [[File:Rampage_Icon.png|sub|link=Junker Queen#Rampage|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junker Queen#Rampage|Rampage]], [[File:Ability-reinhardt5.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Earthshatter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Earthshatter|Earthshatter]], [[File:Ultimate_TerraSurge.png|sub|link=Orisa#Terra Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Terra Surge|Terra Surge]], [[File:DUPLICATE.png|sub|link=Echo#Duplicate|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Echo#Duplicate|Duplicate]] (initial cast), [[File:Ability-reaper3.png|sub|link=Reaper#Shadow Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Shadow Step|Shadow Step]], [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]] and [[File:Ability-mercy5.png|sub|link=Mercy#Resurrect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mercy#Resurrect|Resurrect]]. Activating any of these abilities will also interrupt an active pull. * Following ultimates do not prevent the target from being pulled, but will interrupt an active pull when activated: [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], [[File:Annihilation_Icon.png|sub|link=Ramattra#Annihilation|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ramattra#Annihilation|Annihilation]], [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]], [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] (initial cast), [[File:Ability-winston4.png|sub|link=Winston#Primal Rage|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Primal Rage|Primal Rage]], [[File:Ability-genji4.png|sub|link=Genji#Dragonblade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Dragonblade|Dragonblade]], [[File:Ability-reaper4.png|sub|link=Reaper#Death Blossom|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Death Blossom|Death Blossom]], [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]], [[File:Ability-Lucio4.png|sub|link=Lúcio#Sound Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lúcio#Sound Barrier|Sound Barrier]], [[File:Ability-mercy6.png|sub|link=Mercy#Valkyrie|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mercy#Valkyrie|Valkyrie]], [[File:Ability Moira Coalescence.png|sub|link=Moira#Coalescence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Coalescence|Coalescence]] and [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]]. * Lifegripping a friendly Jetpack Cat will break their tether (whether they are carrying an ally or an enemy). * Allies cannot be pulled through terrain or terrain-like [[objects]], like Mei's [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px raw.official description: Place a tree that instantly heals allies upon sprouting and continues healing periodically as it lives.
raw.heal:
150 (instant)
90 per pulse
raw.fire rate: 1 pulse every 1.75 seconds (8 pulses)
raw.ability details: *After the initial Burst of heal the following pulses will grant the equivalent of 51hp/sec for the remaining tree's lifespan. * Once a hero reaches full health, the tree will convert 100% of its healing to overhealth for up to 100 per hero. ** The overhealth will last for 6 seconds once leaving the AoE healing radius of the tree, or once the duration ends. This allows heroes to have overhealth for up to 21 seconds, starting on the tree's placement. ** While an ally is affected by anti-heal, they will not receive any overhealth (unless they happen to already be at full health). * Can be cancelled early by pressing the ultimate button again. * Can heal for a total of 870 HP per hero. * The AoE healing is line of sight blocked by terrain, but not by [[barrier]]s. ** Tree of Life can still heal through terrain-like objects, like [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]] or another Tree of Life. * The tree is a terrain-like [[object]], which has collision and blocks line of sight. ** [[File:Grappling_claw.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Grappling Claw|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Grappling Claw|Grappling Claw]] and [[File:Ability-widowmaker2.png|sub|link=Widowmaker#Grappling Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Widowmaker#Grappling Hook|Grappling Hook]] can attach to the tree. ** Tree of Life does not block [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] targeting. * Tree of Life can be placed on top of other [[objects]]. If the tree is placed or falls on top of any tangible non-[[barrier]] object other than [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]] another Tree of Life or [[File:Minefield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Minefield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Minefield|Minefield]], it will be destroyed by the tree. This applies to both allied and enemy objects. ** If Tree of Life is placed on [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] or [[File:Jagged_Wall.png|sub|link=Hazard#Jagged Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hazard#Jagged Wall|Jagged Wall]] and the object is destroyed, the tree falls down. ** If Tree of Life is placed on enemy [[File:Minefield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Minefield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Minefield|Minefield]], the mines will activate, damaging the tree. Allied mines will be destroyed without exploding. ** Following objects are destroyed: [[File:Baptiste-ability3.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Immortality Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Immortality Field|Immortality Field]], [[File:Ability-Junkrat2.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Concussion Mine|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Concussion Mine|Concussion Mine]], [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]], [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turret]], [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]], [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]], [[File:Healing_pylon_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Healing Pylon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Healing Pylon|Healing Pylon]] and [[File:Ability-widowmaker3.png|sub|link=Widowmaker#Venom Mine|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Widowmaker#Venom Mine|Venom Mine]]. *** Tree of Life will also prevent pillars from [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] from rising if the wall intersects the tree. ** Tree of Life will push the Mei's [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]]'s weather drone to the side. * Destroys fences and other breakables that collide with the tree. * Tree of Life cannot be [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hacked]], neither directly or by being hit by [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]]. EMP will still damage Tree of Life. * [[Payload]] and [[Push]] [[TS-1|robot]] will phase through the tree if it was placed in their path. ** The Payload can move the tree with them if it was placed on them.

raw.official description: Wall Riding empowers your next Soundwave, increasing its knockback by 25% and damage by 50%.
raw.ability details:
* Wall Riding fills a meter. Approximately 5 seconds of continuous Wall Riding is required for empowerment.
* While charging, the meter starts to shortly decay if Lúcio is not Wall Riding. However, once empowerment is achieved, it can no longer decay and persists in empowered state until Soundwave is used.
* The empowered state is indicated by green soundwaves emitted from Lúcio's weapon.

raw.shot type: [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Jump onto a wall to ride along it.
raw.ability details:
* If Wall Jump On release is enabled, then while on a wall, you can press crouch to fall off it without making a jump.
* To start a Wall Ride, jump at a nearby wall and hold the Jump key. Lúcio will Wall Ride as long as it is held.
* If the option "Allow Backwards Wall Ride" is enabled, Lúcio will be able to start a Wall Ride midair without needing to look at the wall or needing to move prior to Wall Riding.
* Unlike most heroes, Lucio's speed does not decrease while flying with speed higher than 5.5 m/s.
** However, his speed begins to decrease if his speed in the air is more than double his speed on the ground. 
*** With healing aura, slow will occur at speeds above 11 m/s, with speed aura at speeds above 13.76 m/s, with speed aura + Amp It Up at speeds above 17.6 m/s.
* When looking down while wall riding and holding forward to press jump, Lucio will jump up at a speed of 4.12 m/s. If you look up, then the speed will be 9.12 m/s.  However, in this cases, there will be practically no wall leap horizontal speed buff.
* If you look straight ahead during a wall ride and press jump, Lucio will jump up at a vertical speed of 6.54 m/s. By holding forward in advance, you can increase the vertical speed to 6.9 m/s without any penalty.
* Wall Ride's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

shot_type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect

raw.official description: Switches between two songs: Healing Boost heals nearby allies, and Speed Boost makes nearby allies move faster.
raw.heal:
20 per second (allies)
12 per second (self)
raw.ability details:
* Lúcio himself only heals for 60% of the base healing amount.
* Both songs require line of sight and does not affect allies behind enemy [[barrier]]s.
* If a teammate leaves the area, the current song effect lingers for 1 second, unless the song is switched.
* The song can be switched simultaneously while performing any other action.
* There is a short 0.4 second delay before the song swaps. This also limits when the song can be swapped again.
** If "hold to crossfade" option is enabled, visually the aura switches after 0.2 seconds. However, to truly switch the aura, you need to hold down the button for the entire swap delay of 0.4 seconds.
* The aura of speed affects the speed of movement on the surface and walls. Enabling/disabling it while flying does not affect speed unless your speed is greater than 11 m/s.
* Crossfade's [[movement speed]] buff counts towards global movement speed buff cap.
* If Lucio is hacked, Crossfade automatically turns off.

raw.official description: Create temporary extra health for nearby allies.
raw.ability details: * If Lúcio is or becomes airborne while casting Sound Barrier, the cast will not finish until he lands on a walkable surface, unless he is suspended in air by [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]] or [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]], in which case the cast will go through mid-air. ** Lúcio's [[gravity]] is increased to 240% while casting Sound Barrier, causing him to fall faster. ** Lúcio does a small leap upon activation. This increases the cast time by ~0.06 seconds if Lúcio, under normal conditions, lands on the same level of elevation. * When the cast finishes, there is approximately a 0.83 second period where allies can still receive the overhealth if they enter Sound Barrier's radius. * Overhealth immediately starts decaying at a rate of 125 overhealth per second (12 overhealth every 0.096 seconds). * Destroys fences and other breakables in the area.

raw.official description: Gain temporary health when dealing critical damage.
raw.ability details: * After 2 seconds, the [[Hit points#Overhealth|overhealth]] starts decaying at a rate of 5 hp/s. Dealing more critical damage will reset this condition. * Both headshots as well as hitting a burning target with "Cha-Cha" counts toward this. * The center of ''Could not find 'Overrun' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'''s stomp counts as critical damage and gives overhealth accordingly to damage dealt. * Critical damage dealt to objects such as [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]], [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] and others, does not generate overhealth.

raw.official description: Critical hits grant Mauga 3 temporary overhealth on Cardiac Overdrive's next use, up to 150 overhealth.
raw.ability details: * The stored Overhealth resets when Mauga dies.

raw.official description: Overrun ignites enemies hit.
raw.ability details: * Both charge and stomp can apply the burn. * This has the same properties as the ignite from [[File:Incendiary_Chaingun_icon.png|middle|link=Mauga#Incendiary Chaingun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Incendiary Chaingun|Incendiary Chaingun]], except it does not trigger [[File:Pyromaniac.png|sub|link=Mauga#Pyromaniac|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Pyromaniac|Pyromaniac]].

Igniting enemies with Incendiary Chaingun grants 50 overhealth.

abilities.name: Overrun

raw.official description: Charge forward to stomp and launch enemies. You are unstoppable while charging.
raw.cast time: 0.21 + 0.50 seconds
raw.duration:
0.32 seconds (charge, min)
2 seconds (charge, max)
0.6 seconds (stomp)
1 second (knockdown)
raw.ability details: * Cooldown starts when the ability ends. * Mauga cannot shoot or use other abilities (except Cardiac Overdrive) during Overrun. * Mauga can turn while charging using the directional keys. * Overrun's charge ends automatically after 2 seconds of charging, causing Mauga to leap at the end. ** Mauga takes 8 footsteps in this time. * The charge can be canceled into stomp early by pressing the Primary Fire or Jump key. * Alternatively, Overrun can be canceled early by pressing the ability key again, which causes the charge to end without a stomp. ** The automatical stomp at max duration can also be canceled until slightly after the jump has started. ** A manually input stomp cannot be cancelled. * Enemies that collide with Mauga while he is charging take damage and are [[knocked back]]. ** Each enemy can only be hit by the charge once per use. * The stomp has different effects based on the target's proximity upon landing: ** Enemies hit by the outer area, will take damage and are knocked back. ** Enemies hit by the inner area take double damage, and are [[knocked down]] for a brief period, similar to [[Sigma]]'s [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|sub|link=Sigma#Accretion|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Accretion|Accretion]]. *** This counts as a [[critical hit]]: it will trigger [[File:Berserker_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Berserker|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Berserker|Berserker]] and shows in the kill feed as a critical death if it eliminated an enemy. * Overrun's stomp is treated as an actual jump that can clear small gaps. * Unlike [[Reinhardt]]'s [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], Overrun will not stop if directed towards a wall; Mauga will simply keep charging in place. * During the charge, Mauga is immune to being [[hacked]], [[slowed]], [[knocked back]]/up, [[stunned]], [[knocked down]], [[rooted]] or being affected by [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] or ''Could not find 'Cage Fight' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'', excluding the following interactions: ** [[Orisa]] using [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] will be treated as a wall; Mauga will either slide off or stop in place in front of her. ** [[Lifeweaver]] using [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] will temporarily pause the charge until the grip ends, after which he will continue charging from his new position. ** Clashing with another Mauga using Overrun will cause both Maugas to be [[knocked down]] for 1.7 seconds. *** There are no other counter-charge behaviours, such as with [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]], [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]] or [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]]. Overrun will continue, but the aforementioned abilities will stop upon colliding with Mauga during Overrun. **** Due to a bug, Mauga still receives crowd control if hit by [[Sigma|Sigma]]'s [[File:Perk_HyperStrike.png|sub|link=Sigma#Hyper Strike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Hyper Strike|Hyper Strike]] major perk. **In some cases, Mauga can still be affected by [[crowd control]] during the initial startup animation (i.e, before he starts moving). * Stomp is blocked by [[barrier|barriers]] but the charge collision is not. * Overrun's movement speed buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

* Healing from Cardiac Overdrive is not reduced by the global healing reduction passive.
* Healing from Cardiac Overdrive does not generate [[ultimate charge]].

raw.official description: Deploy a barrier that traps yourself and enemies. Automatically reloads Mauga's Chainguns at the start of the ultimate.
raw.ability details: * Can be interrupted by [[crowd control]] abilities during deployment, causing the Ultimate to be wasted. * Can be canceled early by pressing the ability key again. * Activating Cage Fight restores the ammo of [[File:Incendiary_Chaingun_icon.png|middle|link=Mauga#Incendiary Chaingun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Incendiary Chaingun|Incendiary and Volatile Chainguns]] to full. * Creates a cylindrical barrier centered around a device. ** Like all barriers, it can be shot through by allies, and both enemies and allies can walk through it. ** The barrier extends 3 meters below and 9 meters above the device. ** The device is an invulnerable [[object]] with gravity. ** The device - along with the barrier and chained targets - moves along with any object or map element it is standing on and will fall down if they are retracted or destroyed. *** The device can be placed on [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[payload]], [[push]] barricade, moving platforms and cars in [[Oasis]]. * Mauga and enemy heroes within the barrier's bounds will be [[Status effect#Rooted|leashed]] by a chain and become unable move outside the barrier or vertically beyond a ~9 meter spherical radius of the barrier device. ** [[D.Va]]'s [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], [[Junkrat]]'s [[File:Ability-Junkrat5.png|sub|link=Junkrat#RIP-Tire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#RIP-Tire|RIP-Tire]] and [[Ashe]]'s [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] are also affected. ** Enemies who walk into the barrier even after its initial deployment will also become trapped. ** Enemies need line of sight with the barrier device in order to be tethered by the chains. Once attached, the chains stay on even if enemy moves behind a wall or solid terrain. *** [[Barrier]]s, [[Lifeweaver]]'s [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] or [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] and [[Mei]]'s [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] will not prevent the chains from attaching. *** If deployed atop an already risen [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]], it does not attach to anyone below it (due to its height). Anyone already chained will be pulled off of the platform if they ascend on it. ** Mauga still becomes chained, even if there are no nearby enemies to be chained. * Enemies and Mauga will see "TRAPPED" on their screen and their [[movement abilities]] will be disabled. It also cancels most movement abilities. Enemies can still move around normally. ** It doesn't cancel [[Reaper]]'s [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]] if he activated it while outside, but he can't leave once inside. ** [[Sombra]] will be forced out of [[File:Ability Sombra Thermoptic Camo.png|sub|link=Sombra#Stealth|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Stealth|Stealth]] if she was within range. ** Flying enemies will fall to the ground. *** However, certain abilities (such as [[Illari]]'s [[File:Captive_sun_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Captive Sun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Captive Sun|Captive Sun]]) have a jump animation when casting, which cannot be interrupted by the chains. Once the animation ends, they fall back to the ground. ** Only a few abilities let enemies leave the cylinder: *** [[Orisa]]'s [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] removes the chains, allowing her to exit through the ultimate's barrier. *** [[Symmetra]]'s [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]] can let her and allies leave the cylinder. This also extends to the Mauga that casted the ultimate. *** [[Lifeweaver]]'s [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] can pull an ally out of the cylinder, given he's outside of it. This also extends to the Mauga that casted the ultimate. *** Mauga's Overrun and [[Moira]]'s [[File:Ability Moira Fade.png|sub|link=Moira#Fade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Fade|Fade]] can allow them to go through the Cage Fight without being trapped, given their ability does not end while inside. ** [[Sombra]] can teleport out of the cage if her [[File:Ability Sombra Translocator.png|sub|link=Sombra#Translocator|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Translocator|Translocator]] is in flight when she becomes trapped. * Destroying the barrier will end the ultimate early and remove the trapped status effect. * Killing a Mauga using Cage Fight will not remove the barrier.

It will bounce off and deploy on ground. * Ability is stationed wherever it's placed. So, if placed on top of a temporary or moving surface, like Payload, it will remain in midair, even after the surface expires or moves. * [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] destroys any pillar of Ice Wall if placed on it, and will also prevent pillars from rising if Ice Wall is placed intersecting an existing tree. * Destroys fences and other breakables that collide with the pillars.

raw.ability keywords: area of effect;;spherical::armor piercing;;deployable effect::barrier piercing;;deployable::cleansable;;deployable::deployable;;invulnerable::stun;;deployable::travel time
raw.ability details: * Deals up to 85 damage over the duration. * The first tick results in a 50% progress towards the freeze (also inflicts a 50% slow effect), then it continues at a rate of 25% per second (also slows at a rate of 25% per second). (The slow effect is capped at 75%.) At 100% or higher freeze progress, the very next tick freezes the target; therefore, it takes about 2 seconds in order to freeze. ** This means that (using Blizzard alone), targets will only be frozen for 2.25 seconds. * Using [[Endothermic Blaster]]'s primary fire greatly speeds up the freezing process (and will allow targets to be frozen for a little more than 2.25 seconds). * The '''Area of Effect''' is based off line of sight from the deployed drone and blocked by terrain and terrain-like [[objects]], such as [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]]. ** Pierces [[barrier]]s, payloads, and [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. ** If [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] is placed on top of the drone at the center, the drone will be displaced. * The drone can be placed on and lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. * The projectile that deploys the drone acts as a standard barrier-piercing projectile. ** It can be absorbed by '''Negate Projectile''' abilities (such as [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]]) and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]]. ** If the projectile passes through [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], all damage dealt by the ability will be amplified. * Once deployed by the projectile, the drone functions as an indestructible deployable (similar to [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]]), and cannot be negated or damaged. ** The only exception is Sombra's [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]], which destroys the deployable if hit.

shot_type: [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted (ally) [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement

raw.official description: Fly towards an ally. While in flight, Jump launches you forward, and Crouch launches you upward.
raw.ability details:
* Can be used on both living and dead allies.
* While flying, Guardian Angel be cancelled mid-flight by pressing the hotkey once more or extended through a Jump or Crouch input:
** Pressing Jump will allow Mercy to propel herself in the direction she is facing.
** Pressing Jump and back simultaneously will launch Mercy backwards from her target, however this is slower than propelling where you are facing.
** Pressing Crouch will instantly launch Mercy vertically.
* Extending Guardian Angel with a Jump or Crouch input sets its duration to 1 seconds. The extension cannot be canceled.
* The cooldown of Guardian Angel starts when its duration ends.
* A meter shows how much momentum Mercy has during Guardian Angel. The higher the meter, the stronger the jumps.
* Destroys fences and other breakables in the way.

shot_type: [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted (fallen ally)

raw.official description: Revive a dead teammate.
raw.heal: Revives ally at full health
raw.ability details: * Eliminated allies leave souls, appearing as glowing yellow balls, at the location of their death. Targeting a soul with Resurrect starts the cast. * Resurrect is a [[channeled]] ability. Mercy cannot use other abilities while channeling and the cast is interrupted, immediately starting the cooldown without resurrecting, if Mercy is [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]]. * Once Resurrect starts casting, line of sight does not need to be maintained. However, the cast is interrupted if Mercy moves more than 7 meters away from her target while channeling. ** The soul can also be moved away from Mercy by terrain-like [[object]]s, such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. If moved too far, this will also interrupt Resurrect. * Cooldown does not reset when respawning. * The fallen ally is highlighted on the player's screen. * The resurrected players gain 2.25 seconds of invulnerability. Until the end of invulnerability, the resurrected player cannot shoot or use abilities. ** The target can start moving 1.5 seconds after being resurrected. * Does not grant ultimate charge. * [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] cannot be used on Mercy while she is channeling Resurrect. * Fallen allies' souls can be lifted by Lifeweaver's [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. * Using this ability on a falling ally soul will freeze it midair until the resurrection is complete.

After which, the damage will still be boosted even without Mercy boosting the hero. * Mercy gains [[Ultimate ability|ultimate charge]] equal to the damage that she amplifies, except from self-damage. * Mercy's damage boost does not stack with itself.

shot_type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted

raw.official description: Throw your hat to heal an ally, bouncing to nearby allies and healing you when it returns.
raw.range distance:
30 meters (initial throw)
12 meters (bounce range from target)
raw.ability details:
* Bounces up to 3 times, automatically targeting the lowest health ally within range after the first target.
* Cannot bounce back to an ally it has already hit.
* The hat cannot be targeted through terrain, but once the hat has locked on to an ally, it can follow them through walls.
* If no ally is within line of sight of the kasa upon hit, it will automatically return to Mizuki.
* Mizuki will always receive 30 healing from Healing Kasa, regardless of the order it hits him.
* The cooldown starts once the Healing Kasa is thrown, not once it returns to Mizuki.
* If the target ally is eliminated before the hat can reach them, it will instead travel towards the next nearest ally (if in range).
** If no ally is in range, it will return to Mizuki, and provide no self healing.

immune to crowd control [[status effect]]s, including slow, root, knockback, stun, knocked down, gravity and [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack's]] ability lockout. ** Orisa can still be affected by non-cc status effects, including [[damage over time]], [[anti-heal]] and Hack's marking for reveal and [[File:Ability Sombra Opportunist.png|sub|link=Sombra#Opportunist|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Opportunist|Opportunist]] bonus damage. ** Walking through [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]] with Fortify active or activating it after being caught destroys it. ** Orisa takes no damage from [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] or [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]]. * Orisa can be lifted by Lifeweaver's [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] and pulled by the effect ends. * Cooldown starts when the ability is activated. * Can be manually canceled by reactivating the ability.

raw.official description: Orisa temporarily reduces damage she takes and cannot be affected by movement-impairing effects.
raw.ability details: * Reworked in ''Overwatch 2''. * Orisa takes no [[headshot]] damage. * Orisa becomes immune to all movement-related [[Crowd Control]] effects, including stun, sleep, freeze, pin, knock down, knock back, pull, slow, root, etc. ** Orisa can escape [[File:Ability-Junkrat3.png|sub|link=Junkrat#Steel Trap|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#Steel Trap|Steel Trap]], [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], and [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]]. ** Orisa takes no damage from [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] or [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]]. * Orisa can be [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|hacked]] by [[Sombra]] while using Fortify, but this will not interrupt the ability. * Allies behind Orisa are not protected from abilities such as [[File:Ability-reinhardt5.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Earthshatter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Earthshatter|Earthshatter]] or [[File:Ability-Junkrat5.png|sub|link=Junkrat#RIP-Tire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junkrat#RIP-Tire|RIP-Tire]]. * [[Armor]] is applied after damage reduction. Attacks consisting of 16.667... or lower damage (e.g. Sombra's [[File:Ability Sombra Machine Pistol.png|middle|link=Sombra#Machine Pistol|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Machine Pistol|Machine Pistol]]) are reduced by 70%. Beams are reduced by 58%.

abilities.name: Javelin Spin
shot_type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
raw.official description: Spin your javelin to destroy projectiles and block melee attacks, while also pushing enemies and increasing forward speed.
raw.damage:
20 (first hit)
5.3 (subsequent hits)
raw.cast time: 0 + 0.1 seconds
raw.ability keywords: negate projectile::ignore barrier;;negate effect::ignore melee::channeled::speed cap
raw.ability details: * Deals up to 100 damage over the duration. * Orisa cannot shoot or use quick melee during Javelin Spin. * Using [[File:Ability_EnergyJavelin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Energy Javelin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Energy Javelin|Energy Javelin]] interrupts Javelin Spin. * [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] can be used simultaneously with Javelin Spin without interrupting it. * Javelin Spin is a [[channeled]] ability; the ability ends immediately if Orisa becomes [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], putting the ability on cooldown. * Orisa gains a [[movement speed]] bonus while moving forward or diagonally forward while the spin animation is active and a smaller bonus for 2 seconds after it ends. * Cannot block beam, AoE or movement-based attacks, such as [[File:Ability-genji3.png|sub|link=Genji#Swift Strike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Swift Strike|Swift Strike]] or abilities marked as Strong Projectile, such as [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|sub|link=Sigma#Accretion|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Accretion|Accretion]] and [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]]. ** Abilities that create an AoE or leave a deployable, but travel as a projectile can be blocked before they land. ** Cannot block [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]] despite it being marked as a Melee ability, due to its stun interrupting the channel first. * Damage and knockback can be line of sight blocked by [[barrier]]s. (Damaging "through" barriers, like Reinhardt's shield, happens when the hitbox's origin is pushed past it.) Negate Projectile property fully pierces barriers. * Cooldown starts when the spin animation ends. * Can be manually canceled by reactivating the ability. * The ability is incorrectly marked with Movement keyword, it is not interrupted or disabled by [[Hinder]]. * Negate projectile mentions the ability "reflects" projectiles, however it only absorbs them.

raw.ability details: * Cooldown begins immediately upon activation. * The projectile bounces off of walls and terrain-like objects and deploys the barrier upon landing on ground. ** The barrier cannot be deployed on objects with a walkable surface, such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]]. The projective will bounce off of them like walls. * The barrier extends slightly below where it is deployed, for example if it is on a ledge or a payload. * The projectile takes approximately 5.5 seconds to land if fired directly upwards. * Prevents Orisa from firing her Fusion Driver or using Energy Javelin for 0.5 seconds

raw.official description: Orisa throws out a stationary barrier that can protect her and her allies from enemy fire.
raw.ability details: * Replaced by [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]] in ''Overwatch 2''. * Cooldown begins immediately. * The projectile ignores [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], and [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]]. * The [[barrier]] is shaped like a football net. * The barrier extends slightly below where it is deployed in the case that it is on a ledge or a payload. * The barrier can be deployed without interrupting [[File:Ability Orisa Fusion Driver.png|middle|link=Orisa#Fusion Driver|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fusion Driver|Fusion Driver]]'s reload.

raw.ability details:
* Damage per second: 140 while firing (100.88 overall w/ recharge, 86.84 w/ overheat)
* Max spread is reached after 21 shots.
* Uses a heat-based mechanic instead of ammo/reload.
** Fusion Driver generates 2.04% heat per projectile fired.
*** This amounts to 50 projectiles or 5 seconds of continuous fire before overheating.
** While not firing, Fusion Driver instantly starts cooling, recharging 53% heat per second. It takes 1.9 seconds to restore 100% of the heat meter.
** Once overheated, the weapon cannot be used for 3 seconds.
** Abilities can be used without interrupting the recharge even while the weapon is overheated.

18.8/s (periodic damage)
72 - 500 (release)
raw.cast time:
None (initial cast)
0.2 seconds (release)
0.3 seconds (recovery)
raw.ability details: * When activated, Orisa starts generating charge and enemies are pulled toward a point above Orisa and take periodic damage while in the area. ** Charge starts accumulating and Fortify is gained immediately upon button press, but the pull and periodic damage start taking effect after a 0.4 second delay. ** The pull will not stop until the target reaches the point or leaves the area. ** Orisa cannot move, shoot or use any other abilities until the cast is finished. * When released, enemies in the area take damage equal to the charge percentage and are [[knocked back]] depending on the charge. ** Charge starts at 25% and accumulates at a rate of 62.5% per second in the first 2.5 seconds. ** Charge accumulates at a rate of 212.5/s in the last 1.5 seconds. ** Charge can be released after 0.75 seconds (~72%) and will be automatically released upon reaching 500%. *** Including the periodic damage, the total damage is ~83 at minimum charge and 571 at maximum charge. ** The minimum time required to kill a 250hp enemy is ~2.7 seconds (around 210% charge). * Initial pull, periodic damage and final damage all pierce enemy [[barrier]]s, but are line of sight blocked by terrain and terrain-like [[objects]], such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. * Orisa can be lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] (either allied or enemy) during Terra Surge. * [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] cannot be used on Orisa while channeling Terra Surge. Activating Terra Surge will also interrupt an active pull. * Orisa does gains ultimate charge with environmental kill, but require her suicide moment first. ([[Ilios]]'s well is the best possible way to do so.)

raw.ability details: * Transforms into Nemesis form until the Ultimate ends. ** If Annihilation is activated from Nemesis Form, it has no recovery time and Ramattra regains the armor bonus from entering Nemesis Form. ** The cooldown of Nemesis Form is not affected by Annihilation. If Annihilation is activated from Nemesis Form, Nemesis Form's cooldown immediately begins as Annihilation is activated. Making it possible to chain Nemesis Form into Annihilation into another Nemesis Form if Annihilation lasts longer than the cooldown of Nemesis Form. * Annihilation's tether damage by energy swarm is not interrupted by crowd control. * Timer ticks down at 85% reduced rate when any enemy Heroes are affected by Annihilation, resulting in a maximum duration of 20 seconds. ** Enemies must be within line of sight to be affected by Annihilation. ** Does not affect enemies through [[Barrier|barriers]], including [[Zarya]]'s [[File:Ability-zarya2.png|sub|link=Zarya#Particle Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Particle Barrier|Particle Barrier]] and [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Projected Barrier]], or terrain-like [[object]]s, like [[Mei]]'s [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]]. ** Does not affect invulnerable enemies, like [[Zenyatta]] during [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]]. ** [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] counts as a valid target. Constructs, such as [[Symmetra]]'s [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turrets]] and [[Torbjorn]]'s [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Deploy Turret]], do not take damage. ** Does not affect [[Sombra]] while she is [[Stealth|invisible]], unless Ramattra enters her detection radius. * Can be canceled, by (re)activating Nemesis Form ability. This can be changed in settings.
