raw.official description: Significantly reduces damage taken from the front and reduces movement speed.
raw.ability details: * Exclusive to Nemesis form. * Cooldown starts when blocking is stopped. * No maximum duration; only limited by the duration of Nemesis Form. * Block is a [[channeled]] ability; the ability is interrupted when Ramattra is affected by [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], including subsidiary effects. ** The impact damage from the instance that applied the effect will still be blocked. * Damage is blocked if the horizontal angle between the direction Ramattra is facing and the direction from the impact location to its source is less than 53.15 degrees. ** Ramattra's vertical view angle does not affect blocking. * Effectiveness extends to area abilities and splash damage from projectiles. Damage is generally blocked when facing the center of the AoE. ** Damage from [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]] and [[File:Ravenous_Vortex_Icon.png|sub|link=Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|Ravenous Vortex]] is blocked by facing the hero that used it instead of the AoE center. ** Damage from [[File:Ability-hanzo4.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Dragonstrike|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Dragonstrike|Dragonstrike]] and [[File:Ability-torbjorn5.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Molten Core|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Molten Core|Molten Core]] is blocked regardless of facing direction. ** Does not reduce damage from [[Sombra]]'s [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] or Sigma's [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] (incl. both lift and slam). * Block mitigates delayed or triggered detonation damage from stuck projectiles and debuffs, regardless of the direction Ramattra is facing. ** Following abilities are affected: [[File:A-36_Icon.png|sub|link=Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Bastion#A-36 Tactical Grenade|A-36 Tactical Grenade]], [[File:STICKY BOMBS.png|sub|link=Echo#Sticky Bombs|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Echo#Sticky Bombs|Sticky Bombs]], [[File:Ability-tracer4.png|sub|link=Tracer#Pulse Bomb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Pulse Bomb|Pulse Bomb]] and [[File:Captive_sun_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Captive Sun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Captive Sun|Captive Sun]]. * Does not affect [[damage over time]] effects from abilities. * Hides head hitbox from the front. * Block overrides the global 50% damage reduction buff cap.

raw.official description: Create a barrier at the targeted location.
raw.ability details: * Exclusive to Omnic form. * Hold to target. While targeting, a line on the floor is generated that shows the player where the barrier will be drawn when the button is released. ** Targeting can be canceled by pressing Primary Fire or Escape. ** Only one barrier can be active at a time. Creating a new one replaces the previous. *** A new barrier cannot be created near the location of an active Void Barrier. * Ability is stationed wherever it's placed. So, if placed on top of a temporary or moving surface, like [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] or the [[Payload]], it will remain in midair, even after the surface expires or moves. * Ability cooldown begins the moment the barrier is placed, similar to [[File:Ability-winston3.png|sub|link=Winston#Barrier Projector|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Barrier Projector|Barrier Projector]].

raw.ability details: * Replaced in [[Season/2026|Season 1: Conquest]] with the Stalwart sub-role. * Reduces the initial velocity from knock back effects by 30%. For example, [[File:Ability-Lucio5.png|sub|link=Lúcio#Soundwave|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lúcio#Soundwave|Soundwave]] sends its victims back 17 m/s, but for Reinhardt, it is 11.9 m/s. * The distance in which Reinhardt is knocked back is NOT reduced by 30%; it is reduced by more. This is because the velocity has to accelerate back to normal; a higher initial velocity means more time at higher velocities and more time with a velocity in general. * Does not affect [[File:Ability Orisa Halt!.png|sub|link=Orisa#Halt!|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Halt!|Halt!]], [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]], [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], or [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] because they are not knock back effects.

abilities.name: Charge

raw.shot type:
[[File:Quick_melee.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Melee|Melee ]] Melee
[[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Charge forward and smash an enemy against a wall.
raw.cast time:
0.6 + 0.5 seconds (wall impact)
0.6 + 0.1 seconds (canceled)
raw.duration:
3 seconds (charge, max)
0.3 seconds (charge, min)
0.65 seconds (stun on wall hit)
raw.ability details: * Reinhardt can cancel the charge by pressing the ability button again. * Reinhardt can turn while charging but his control is reduced. * If Charge directly collides with an enemy, that enemy is "pinned" (stunned), and if they hit a wall, the pinned target takes the wall hit damage. If Charge ends before hitting a wall, no damage is inflicted, but the target will remain stunned for an additional 0.65 seconds. Only 1 enemy can be pinned at a time. * If Charge indirectly collides with an enemy, or if there is already a target pinned, the enemy is knocked away and takes 50 damage. * Colliding with any immovable entity, such as Orisa during [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]] or [[File:Overrun_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Overrun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Overrun|Overrun]], is also treated as a wall impact. ** Colliding with deployables is also treated as a wall impact, but also deals half of Reinhardt's pin damage to the deployable. *** Charging into Illari's healing pylon disables it for a short time. * Abilities that make the target [[phased out]], like [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]] or [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]], can prevent a target from being pinned, but once hit the effect can only be removed by [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]. * Abilities that make the target [[invincible]], like [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Projected Barrier]] or [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]], do not prevent or remove the pinned effect, but can prevent the target from taking damage. * If Charge collides with [[File:Rocket_punch.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Rocket Punch|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Rocket Punch|Rocket Punch]], [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]], a charging [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]], or another ''Could not find 'Charge' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'', both participants get [[Crowd Control#Knock Down|knocked down]] for 1.7 seconds. Any pinned targets will be damaged as if they hit a wall. * Charge can break tether effects (except [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]] because it disables movement abilities). * Reinhardt's visible character model is shifted to the right in the Charge animation, causing his left shoulder, rather than the middle of his body, to be the center of his hitbox. Due to this disparity, players should focus on the crosshair position over the character model when aiming. ** Tl;dr: Left shoulder pins, right shoulder boops * Charge's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

0.65 seconds (stun on wall hit)

raw.ability details: * Can be used while airborne. * The hitbox is blocked by enemy barriers, however it is possible to hit "through" them if the origin of the hitbox, i.e. your barrier, travels past theirs. * Shield Slam does '''not''' have the stalemate behavior, where colliding with other charging abilities, like [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]] and [[File:Shield Bash.png|sub|link=Brigitte#Shield Bash|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Brigitte#Shield Bash|Shield Bash]], causes a knockdown.

raw.official description: Hold [[File:Key mouse 2.png|20x20px|link=]] to deploy a frontal energy barrier.
raw.ability details: * Using ''Could not find 'Fire Strike' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'', ''Could not find 'Charge' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' or ''Could not find 'Earthshatter' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' automatically puts down the barrier to cast the ability. To use Rocket Hammer, the barrier must be first manually released. * Barrier health regenerates at a rate of 150 per second after being down for 2 seconds. * Barrier Field is a channeled ability, If Reinhardt is hacked, slept, stunned and knocked down, it will be released. ** Holding secondary fire also raises back the barrier automatically when the cast resolves if Fire Strike or Earthshatter was used. * The center of shield relative to Reinhard is shifted ~0.075 m to the left. * Changing the direction of the character's gaze (and hence the shield zone) takes time. It takes ~0.14 seconds to be able block something what was exactly behind you. * By holding the primary fire key, Reinhardt can look around at his surroundings without turning. * The barrier cracks once at 50% and again at 25%. At 20%, Reinhardt will [[Reinhardt/Quotes#Abilities|say]] his barrier is failing.

abilities.name: Earthshatter

raw.shot type: [[File:Tectonic Shock icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Shockwave|Shockwave ]] Shockwave
raw.official description: Knock down all enemies in front of you.
raw.ability details: * Requires line of sight. ** Earthshatter will be stopped by barriers, even if the damage from Earthshatter breaks the barrier. * Earthshatter has a small amount of vertical height (1.77 meters), allowing it to affect enemies in the air. ** This distance is calculated as the difference between the height of the enemy and the height of the surface over them. ** By jumping off the payload at the right time, you can usually gain enough height to avoid being hit. * Earthshatter is a channeled ability and it is interrupted by becoming hacked, stunned or knocked down. * Earthshatter deals an additional 150 damage within 1.75m of the impact area. This counts as a [[critical hit]]. * While casting Earthshatter Reinhardt ingnores collision with other heroes. * If Reinhardt is/becomes airborne while casting Earthshatter, the cast will not finish until he hits the ground. * Reinhardt is unaffected by [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]] while casting Earthshatter. * [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] cannot be used on Reinhardt while he is casting Earthshatter. Activating Earthshatter will also interrupt an active pull.

abilities.name: Chain Hook

raw.shot type:
[[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile
[[File:Whip_Shot.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Melee|Melee ]] Extended Melee
raw.official description: Drag a targeted enemy to you.
raw.cast time: 0.1 + 0.2 seconds
raw.ability details: * Cooldown starts when Chain Hook ends. * Hooked targets are released if they leave Roadhog’s line of sight before being pulled. * Chain Hook is a [[channeled]] ability; Roadhog cannot shoot or use other abilities while it is active, and it ends immediately if Roadhog becomes [[stunned]], [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], including their subsidiary effects. * If an enemy hero is hit, there is a ~0.3 seconds delay before the pull activates. During this time, there must be an unobstructed line of sight from the head of Roadhog to the head of the enemy. ** Once this timer ends and the hook starts pulling an enemy towards him, the LoS check is disabled and the target's end location is determined: ** The final stun when the target stops moving is 0.4 seconds. *** The target is pulled at a 3 meter distance from Roadhog, unless it was already closer when hit. *** The end location is shifted horizontally in the direction Roadhog is facing at the end of the timer, up to 70 degree rotation from the location where Hook connected. * Roadhog can start shooting as soon as his chain is gone. ** Once he is able to shoot, the target will lose their stun after 0.192 seconds. ** The minimum stun time if the ability was not interrupted is 0.7 seconds. ** The maximum stun time if Roadhog and his target are stationary is ~1.2 seconds. * Damage is inflicted the instant the hook lands. If this damage is enough to kill the target, the hook will return instantly to Roadhog without holding him in place as it usually does on a hit. * Chain Hook is blocked but not reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]]. * A self-destructing D.Va mech can be hooked. * Enemies being carried by [[File:Ability Lifeline.png|sub|link=Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Jetpack Cat#Lifeline|Lifeline]] can be hooked. The tether will break. * Targets can be pulled out of Chain Hook by [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]. * Chain Hook immediately ends if Roadhog is pulled by [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] any time while casting it.

raw.official description: Launch a trap that slows and damages nearby enemies.
raw.cast time: 0.14 + 0.4 seconds
raw.ability details: * ''Removed in Season 18.'' * Cooldown starts when the ability is activated. * The trap is an [[object]]. ** The trap deploys upon landing on object or terrain with a walkable surface, and bounces off when colliding with other surfaces or a hero. ** Only one trap can be active at a time. An upward arrow above the ability icon indicates that a trap is active. ** The trap can be destroyed while dormant but not while it is in air or once it has been triggered. ** The trap is visible to all players. ** Turrets and [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] do not target the trap. * The trap stays on ground until destroyed, a new trap is deployed or until an enemy hero (incl. [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]) enters its radius triggering it. ** There is a short delay after deploying before the trap can trigger. ** The player receives a cue when an enemy triggers or destroys the trap. ** Enemies that are [[phased out]], such as Reaper during [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]], cannot trigger the trap. ** Enemies that are [[invulnerable]], such as Zenyatta during [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]], can trigger the trap. ** [[Invisible]] enemies can trigger the trap. * Upon triggering all enemies within the radius take damage and receive a small [[knock up]]. The trap then slows all enemies within a larger area and deals periodic damage to enemies while they stay within the radius. ** The slow stacks with other slows, such as [[File:Ravenous_Vortex_Icon.png|sub|link=Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ramattra#Ravenous Vortex|Ravenous Vortex]] and [[File:Ability-mei1.png|middle|link=Mei#Endothermic Blaster|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Endothermic Blaster|Endothermic Blaster]]. * The trap can be lifted by [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] and [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] if the trap is placed on them afterwards, but they cannot lift a trap that is already on ground. ** The AoE is also lifted with the trap if it was placed on the platform/wall. * Barriers block the projectile and prevent a trap on the ground from triggering through them, but the damage pierces barriers. * If the cast time for throwing the trap is interrupted, the trap will not be thrown and the ability will go on cooldown. * The trap cannot be targeted by [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]], and [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] only damages the trap. * The trap is destroyed if [[payload]], [[TS-1|push robot]] or push barricade travels over it. * The trap is destroyed if [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] is deployed or falls on top of it. * The projectile travels the furthest when thrown at a 35 degree angle.

raw.ability details: * Bike can be steered by aiming and sharper turns can be made with left and right directional inputs. The bike can jump, and pressing backward button can reduce the bike movement speed. * Launching the bike while in air, has a short targeting period, during which the jump gains some extra height, and Shion can move laterally at regular walking speed and redirect the launch. Holding the button extends the targeting period for up 1.5 seconds. * Activating [[File:Ability Evade.png|sub|link=Shion#Evade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Shion#Evade|Evade]] or ''Could not find 'Satsuriku Spree' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' while riding dismounts Shion and launches the bike along the ground. [[File:Ability Execution.png|sub|link=Shion#Execution|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Shion#Execution|Execution]] cannot be activated while riding. ** During the aerial targeting, [[File:Ability Evade.png|sub|link=Shion#Evade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Shion#Evade|Evade]] cannot be used and activating ''Could not find 'Satsuriku Spree' in 'Abilities'. Check spelling and see [[Help:Cargo]] if the issue persists.'' will completely remove the bike instead. * When launched along ground, the bike can slide along the walls if it hits them at low angle. Aerial launch will cause teh bike to explode immediately upon contact with terrain. * Any interception ability like [[stun]], [[hinder]], [[sleep]] etc. against Joyride will cancel her movement but automatically dismount her bike forward. ** However, hack will also remove the bike directly. ** [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]] will dismount the bike on the ground. However it will completely remove bike if Shion in the air. * Reloads [[File:Ability Kira Pistols.png|middle|link=Shion#Kira Pistols|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Shion#Kira Pistols|Kira Pistols]] after riding for 1.5 seconds. Aerial launch targeting period does not count towards this.

raw.official description: Rush forward 3 times in a storm of gunfire.
raw.ability details: * Primary fire, quick melee and Shion's other abilities are disabled while Satsuriku Spree is active. * While active, pressing primary fire activates "Rush-Burst", launching Shion in the target direction before dealing a burst of damage to all nearby enemies, pressing secondary fire activates "Burst" which causes has the same effect but without dashing. * During the burst, Shion locks on to all players and [[deployables]] (barriers, turrets, traps, etc.) in its range that are within line of sight, and fires toward each target at the same time. ** Targets can be hit by shots aimed at targets behind them, causing them to block it and take the damage instead. ** Barriers block shots, but do not prevent line of sight for acquiring targets. * Being [[hindered]] or otherwise locked out of movement abilities, such as during [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]'s grace period, will cause Rush-Burst to instead activate Burst. * Being [[hacked]] temporarily disables both Rush-Burst and Burst, but does not end the ability.

raw.official description: Launch an Anchor Drone. Reactivate to launch towards it.
raw.ability details: * When the drone is deployed, Sierra gains 2 reactivation charges that can be used to grapple to the drone. * The cooldown begins on deployment, not on the drones destruction. * Grappling to the drone requires Line of Sight to it, whilst in range. ** The grapple will break if something obstructs the path while grappling toward it. * The drone can be destroyed early by using the Interact key (default F on keyboard). * Firing the drone into environmental cliffs will instantly destroy it, using the cooldown. * Holding the ability button will deploy the drone and immediately grapple to it once. * Momentum is kept after grappling, slightly launching the hero. ** Jumping while grappling will end the grapple early and still launch the hero. * [[File:Helix Rifle.png|sub|link=Sierra#Helix Rifle|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sierra#Helix Rifle|Helix Rifle]] and other abilities can be used whilst grappling. * Whilst [[Hindered]], the drone can be deployed but the grapple cannot be used. * The drone cannot be [[Hacked]] or [[Stunned]], but it can be destroyed.

** Hitting a [[Barrier]] will deal the same damage to it but prevent the shockwave.

raw.official description: Marks an enemy. Helix Rifle shots automatically track marked enemies.
raw.ability details: * After a successful hit on an enemy, [[File:Helix Rifle.png|sub|link=Sierra#Helix Rifle|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sierra#Helix Rifle|Helix Rifle]] is automatically reloaded and its spread is automatically reduced to its minimum value for Tracking Shot's duration while the tracked enemy is within Line of Sight. * Can be reactivated to remove the mark. * The initial projectile can be critically hit, however any tracking shots cannot. * While an enemy is marked: ** All shots fired from [[File:Helix Rifle.png|sub|link=Sierra#Helix Rifle|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sierra#Helix Rifle|Helix Rifle]] whilst the mark is in Line of Sight will fire towards them, as long as you are aiming within approximately 90 degrees of the target. ** Any tracking shots will not use ammo. ** A visual UI mark will be on the target, visible even behind cover. *** This becomes red and smaller when the target is in Line of Sight, whilst white and larger if they're behind cover. * The initial marking projectile can be deflected with [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but the auto-aim properties will not apply. * The projectile is Sticky and functions similarly to [[File:Jagged_Blade_Icon.png|sub|link=Junker Queen#Jagged Blade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Junker Queen#Jagged Blade|Jagged Blade]] or [[File:Ability-tracer4.png|sub|link=Tracer#Pulse Bomb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Tracer#Pulse Bomb|Pulse Bomb]]. ** Phasing abilities, such as [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Swift Step|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Swift Step|Swift Step]] or [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]] will remove the mark. ** Cleansing abilities (that do not Phase), such as [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Projected Barrier]] or [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]], will not remove the mark.

raw.official description: Deploy a drone that flies forward and drops explosives.
raw.ability details: * The bombs are deployed from an indestructible drone, that will spread them out in 8 rows of 3, with the final row having only 2. * During deployment, the bombs are considered standard projectiles and can be blocked by [[barriers]] or negated by abilities such as [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]] or [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]]. ** Once they hit a surface, the bombs are primed and explode after a 1 second delay. * The drone will travel a minimum of ~2m height above the ground, regardless of the angle deployed. * The drone will always travel diagonally if the player angles it too far up or down. * Both the drone and the projectiles can be destroyed with [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] prior to detonating, regardless of whether the projectiles are in motion or on a surface.

raw.official description: 40% of Hyperspheres' damage restores Experimental Barrier's Health.
raw.ability details: * The perk can only be activated upon damaging heroes.

raw.official description: Absorbing projectiles with Kinetic Grasp also reduces Accretion's cooldown.
raw.ability details:
* Cooldown reduction is granted when Kinetic Grasp successfully ends. Similar to overhealth conversion, no cooldown reduction is gained if the ability is interrupted.
* Reduction amount is linear to the amount damage negated, with every 100 damage reducing the cooldown by 2 seconds.

raw.official description: Accretion's knockdown duration increases to up to 3 seconds based on travel distance.
raw.ability details:
* ''Removed in Season 18.''
* Duration increase is based on direct distance between the cast location and the impact location.
** As such, a rock launched directly upwards does not gain increased knockdown duration albeit having traveled a much longer distance than required.
* Increase starts at 13 meters and reaches the maximum (+2.2 seconds) at 30 meters distance.

The width and depth roughly match the swirly area, but heightwise it extends downwards to the level of Sigma's feet. * Cannot block Beam, Melee, Extended Melee or AoE attacks or Strong Projectiles. ** Abilities that create an AoE or leave a deployable, but travel as a projectile can generally be blocked before they land. (Includes [[ultimate abilities]] such as [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]], [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]], No results, etc.) ** Following attacks count as strong projectiles: raw.ability details: * Damage per second: ~80.4 while firing * Bounces off walls, floors, and ceilings. Hitting a [[heroes|hero]] or an [[object]] causes the charges to implode. * The charges automatically implode after travelling 22 meters along the trajectory. This effect counts as a [[knockback]]. * Projectiles can be blocked by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], No results, [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]], [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] before they implode. The implosion itself is unaffected. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. abilities.name: Experimental Barrier

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile
raw.official description: Hold to propel a floating barrier; release to stop. Press again to recall the barrier to you.
raw.ability details: * On default settings, the barrier moves forward by holding down the ability key and stops when released. * Barrier health starts to regenerate at a rate of 79.3 per second after being recalled for 2 seconds. * Deploying the barrier is a [[channeled]] action; use of fire and abilities is limited, and being [[stunned]] or [[knocked down]] or [[hacked]], including their subsidiary effects, interrupts the cast. ** Sigma cannot use his primary fire or normal abilities while deploying the barrier. No results can be used, which will upon activation stop the barrier at its current location. ** Being stunned or knocked down stops the barrier at its current location, but will not cause an active barrier to be retracted. * The barrier is automatically recalled if Sigma is eliminated or [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|hacked]] at any time. * Walls will block the barrier's path if the center of the barrier is blocked. The barrier will coast along the ground if the angle down is 45° or lower. * The barrier can be removed by pressing the ability key again, keeping all its remaining health. This can be done at any time, including in the middle of other abilities such as Kinetic Grasp. * The barrier is destroyed if it goes out of bounds. * The barrier cannot be deployed while using [[Quick Melee]].

abilities.name: Gravitic Flux
shot_type: [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement

raw.shot type:
[[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect
[[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Manipulate gravity to lift enemies into the air and slam them back down.
raw.cast time:
0 + 0.6 seconds (activation)
1.0 + 0 seconds (lift)
raw.duration:
5 seconds (targeting)
1 second (activation delay)
2.5 seconds (lift)
0.6 seconds (high gravity)
raw.ability details: * Gravitic Flux's targeting area is cylindrical: Its bottom is a flat 8m radius circle situated 5.5m below the targeted (ground) location and the upper limit of the area is defined by a 15.5m radius sphere centered on this point at the bottom, resulting in ~8-10m upward range from the ground level. * Targeting is based off line-of-sight from the dark center circle of the selected area. Any enemies who can block line of sight from the center, such as by hiding behind a wall, will not be affected by the initial lift. ** Gravitic Flux will target enemies through [[barriers]]. * The selected area is visible to the enemy team briefly before the effect activates. ** Gravitic Flux’s Area of Effect is slightly larger than the marker indicates, so enemies within 0.5-1 meter of the edge of the indicator will also be affected. * Does not affect deployed [[objects]], such as turrets or D.Va's mech during [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], or the map objective. * If no enemies are lifted the effect ends immediately. However, if all lifted enemies escape the suspension or are eliminated, the ability will continue until the end. * Affected enemies are [[rooted]], disabling all [[movement abilities]]. * Gravitic Flux's lift cannot be prevented or removed by effects that grant invincibility or phased out, such as [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Personal/Projected Barrier]], [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]] or [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]], but the damage portions can be prevented. ** Mauga's [[File:Overrun_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Overrun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Overrun|Overrun]] and Venture's [[File:Burrow_icon.png|sub|link=Venture#Burrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Venture#Burrow|Burrow]] can prevent the hero from being lifted, however they cannot remove the effect. Once the heroes have been lifted, these abilities can't be used as they are [[movement abilities]]. ** Heroes affected by No results can use Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]], however, it will not remove the effect. ** Orisa can prevent and remove the effect using [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]]. ** D.Va can remove the effect from herself using [[File:Ability-dva4.png|sub|link=D.Va#Self-Destruct|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Self-Destruct|Self-Destruct]], leaving the exploding mech in the suspension. *** Re-entering mech will also allow D.Va to escape the suspension, however, as [[File:Ability-dva6.png|sub|link=D.Va#Call Mech|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Call Mech|Call Mech]] will be disabled during the suspension, it must be activated before being hit by Gravitic Flux for this to happen. ** Lifeweaver can pull targets out of Gravitic Flux using [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]. * The slam deals damage equal to 50% of the target's maximum HP, which is increased by temporary HP, such as [[overhealth]] or [[armor]], gained from abilities. ** If the ability is interrupted before the slam or a target escapes the suspension the target(s) will fall down naturally without taking damage. ** Slam damage ignores all [[damage modification]]s, both on Sigma and the targets. * Sigma can fly while Gravitic Flux is active. Holding the Jump key causes Sigma to rise, and holding Crouch makes him descend. ** Being [[rooted]] or [[hindered]] make Sigma temporarily unable to fly, but do not prevent the activation or immediately end the ability. ** Gravitic Flux is a channeled ability and it is interrupted by becoming hacked, stunned, knocked down or asleep. ** Vertical and horizontal speed are related. Sigma cannot move faster than 7.15 meters per second of horizontal and vertical speed in same time. *** If the vertical speed is <0.25, then the horizontal maximum can be no more than 7.15 and vice versa. *** If your crosshair is exactly centered and you pinch forward/back and jump/ctrl then your vertical and horizontal speed will be 5.06 meters per second. *** If your crosshair is exactly centered and you pinch forward/back and jump/ctrl and right/left then your vertical speed = 3.58 and horizontal = 6.19.

raw.shot type: [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: When out of combat, become invisible and move faster.
raw.ability details: * Stealth starts on a shorter 1 second cooldown upon respawning. * Sombra automatically turns [[invisible]] 3.5 seconds after not taking an offensive action, receiving damage or being affected by crowd control. ** Entering stealth has a fade out period, during which Sombra is still partially visible. ** Following actions count as "offensive": activating primary fire, [[quick melee]], [[File:Ability Sombra Virus.png|sub|link=Sombra#Virus|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Virus|Virus]] or [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]] or using [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]] on an enemy Hero. *** Excluding Hack, these actions will immediately take Sombra out of stealth upon the key press, but they will not be initiated, unless the key is held or pressed again once Sombra has completely faded in. **** There is no fade in delay if stealth is broken within 0.75 seconds of entering stealth. **** Pressing the Hack button does not interrupt stealth. Only when Hack targets an enemy hero it will immediately take Sombra out of stealth. Additionally, Hack's cast time starts immediately and does not have to wait for Sombra to fade in. ** Using [[File:Ability Sombra Translocator.png|sub|link=Sombra#Translocator|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Translocator|Translocator]] or hacking a [[health pack]] or [[object]] do not interrupt stealth. * If an enemy [[hero]] comes within 4 meters of Sombra, they can "detect" Sombra. When detected, Sombra will remain stealthed, but is visible to that specific enemy hero. This is indicated by a "DETECTED!" message, shown at the top of Sombra's screen. ** [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] cannot detect Sombra. * Sombra becomes "revealed" if she uses [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]] on an [[object]] or [[health pack]] or can be seen through abilities, such as [[File:Ability-hanzo2.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|Sonic Arrow]] or [[File:Ability-widowmaker4.png|sub|link=Widowmaker#Infra-Sight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Widowmaker#Infra-Sight|Infra-Sight]]. When revealed, Sombra will remain in stealth, but Sombra is visible to the entire enemy team. This is indicated by a "REVEALED" message shown at the top of Sombra's screen. * [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted enemy abilities and following auto-targeting attacks cannot target Sombra, unless she is detected by the player or revealed: [[File:Ability-mccree4.png|sub|link=Cassidy#Deadeye|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Cassidy#Deadeye|Deadeye]], [[File:icon-ability.tacticalvisor.png|sub|link=Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|Tactical Visor]], [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Grasp_Alt fire.png|middle|link=Moira#Biotic Grasp Alt Fire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Grasp Alt Fire|Biotic Grasp Alt Fire]] and [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Orb.png|sub|link=Moira#Biotic Orb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Orb|Biotic Orb]] (damage). ** Zenyatta's Orb of Discord will remain attached to Sombra while she is stealthed, as long as there are no obstacles between them, but it will not indicate her location. * Following enemy [[object]]s cannot auto-target Sombra, unless she has been revealed: [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turret]] or [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]. * [[Torbjörn]]'s [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Turret]] can only attack Sombra when she is out of stealth. * Sombra can activate [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] while stealthed. * Sombra cannot contest map [[objective]]s while stealthed. ** If Sombra enters the objective area stealthed, she will remain in stealth. ** If Sombra enters the objective area unstealthed, the cooldown of stealth will be paused until she leaves the objective area. *** The remaining cooldown will be set to 1 second if it was less than that. * Sound effect and voiceover distance is 15 meters. * Stealth's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap.

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability-dva7.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Projectile|Projectile ]] Projectile
raw.official description: Infect enemies with a projectile that deals damage over time. Virus damages hacked targets at a faster rate.
raw.damage:
35 (impact, normal)
70 (impact, hacked)
75 (DoT)
raw.ability details: * Virus's impact damage on hacked targets is doubled and the damage over time is dealt at double rate if the target is hacked. ** The total DoT amount remains the same whether the target is hacked or not or if they become hacked or lose the debuff after the ability has impacted, but the rate and duration of the effect are adjusted accordingly. ** The hacked effect does not have to be from the same Sombra that applied the Virus. For example, hitting Virus on a target hacked by Echo that has copied Sombra also triggers the benefits. * [[Objects]] can also be damaged, and if they are hackable, they can also be damaged faster. * Virus cannot [[headshot]]. However, eliminating a hacked hero with the impact damage shows in the kill feed as a critical hit. * Impact damage is affected by [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] but damage over time is not. * Virus does not benefit from Opportunist, but does from Hack.

abilities.name: Translocator
shot_type: [[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement

raw.shot type:
[[File:Abilities-sigma4.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Arcing projectile|Arcing projectile ]] Arcing projectile
[[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement
raw.official description: Throw a beacon and teleport to it. Become stealthed for a short duration after teleporting.
raw.ability details: * Cooldown for Translocator begins upon teleporting to it. * Once thrown, the translocator activates immediately upon colliding with terrain or a terrain-like object, such as [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], or after 0.25 seconds have passed. ** This results in effective range of 18 meters along the trajectory. ** Translocator destroys breakables, such as fences, while in flight and does not activate upon hitting them. * The translocator cannot be damaged, and it does not collide with heroes or objects. * Being [[stunned]], [[knocked down]], [[hacked]], [[rooted]] or [[hindered]] prevents Translocator from being used. ** If Sombra becomes is affected by aforementioned effects (including [[File:Ability-Roadhog3.png|sub|link=Roadhog#Chain Hook|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Roadhog#Chain Hook|Chain Hook]] and [[File:Ability-reinhardt3.png|sub|link=Reinhardt#Charge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reinhardt#Charge|Charge]]) or caught in [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]] while the Translocator is in air, the teleport will go through and the negative effects will be removed. ** If Sombra becomes affected by Sigma's [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]], the teleport goes through but its effects will not be removed. * Translocator cannot be used while pulled by [[File:Life_Grip_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Life Grip|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Life Grip|Life Grip]]. ** If Sombra was pulled while Translocator is in air, she will still teleport to it, interrupting the pull. * Sombra becomes [[phased out]] for 0.4 seconds after teleporting, making her immune to damage as well as removing and preventing most negative effects. * After teleporting, Sombra gains stealth after a 0.25 second fade out period. Once the duration ends or stealth is interrupted it has a 0.3 second fade in period. Sombra is partially visible while fading in/out. * Stealth is interrupted if Sombra uses primary fire, [[quick melee]], [[File:Ability Sombra Virus.png|sub|link=Sombra#Virus|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Virus|Virus]], [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]], or tries to [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|hack]] an enemy hero. ** These actions will immediately cause Sombra to start to fade in upon the key press, but they will not be initiated, unless the key is held or pressed again once Sombra has completely faded in. *** There is no fade in delay if stealth is broken within 0.75 seconds of entering stealth. * Sombra becomes "revealed" if she uses [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]], takes damage or can be seen through abilities, such as [[File:Ability-hanzo2.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|Sonic Arrow]] or [[File:Ability-widowmaker4.png|sub|link=Widowmaker#Infra-Sight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Widowmaker#Infra-Sight|Infra-Sight]]. When revealed, Sombra will remain in stealth, but Sombra is visible to the entire enemy team. This is indicated by a "REVEALED" message shown at the top of Sombra's screen. ** Taking damage reveals Sombra for 1.3 seconds. * If an enemy [[hero]] comes within 4 meters of Sombra, they can "detect" Sombra. When detected, Sombra will remain stealthed, but is visible to that specific enemy hero. This is indicated by a "DETECTED!" message, shown at the top of Sombra's screen. ** [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] cannot detect Sombra. * [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted enemy abilities and following auto-targeting attacks cannot target Sombra, unless she is detected by the player or revealed: [[File:Ability-mccree4.png|sub|link=Cassidy#Deadeye|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Cassidy#Deadeye|Deadeye]], [[File:icon-ability.tacticalvisor.png|sub|link=Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Soldier: 76#Tactical Visor|Tactical Visor]], [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Grasp_Alt fire.png|middle|link=Moira#Biotic Grasp Alt Fire|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Grasp Alt Fire|Biotic Grasp Alt Fire]] and [[File:Ability Moira Biotic Orb.png|sub|link=Moira#Biotic Orb|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Moira#Biotic Orb|Biotic Orb]] (damage). ** Zenyatta's Orb of Discord will remain attached to Sombra while she is stealthed, as long as there are no obstacles between them, but it will not indicate her location. However, Translocator's teleportation will remove the effect if it was applied before it. * Following enemy [[object]]s cannot auto-target Sombra, unless she has been revealed: [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turret]] or [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]]. * [[Torbjörn]]'s [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Turret]] can only attack Sombra when she is out of stealth. * Sombra can activate [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] while stealthed. * Sombra cannot contest map [[objective]]s while stealthed. ** If Sombra enters the objective area stealthed, she will remain in stealth. * Stealth's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap. * Movement speed buff is tied to the stealth effect. The buff is retained while detected or revealed, but ends if stealth is interrupted.

raw.ability details: * Reworked in Season 7 of ''[[Overwatch 2]]''. * Cooldown for Translocator begins immediately upon throwing it, or when it is destroyed. * Can be manually destroyed using the Interact key. Destroying it this way does not reset the cooldown. * The translocator can neither be thrown or activated while [[rooted]] or [[hindered]]. * The Translocator bounces off walls but not slopes. * Translocator can be placed on and lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] if the latter was deployed first. ** If a translocator is placed on Petal Platform and the platform is destroyed, the translocator is destroyed as well. * Sombra has a very brief moment of invincibility upon translocating, removing most negative effects from Sombra. ** Removes damage over time effects, such as [[File:Ability-ashe2.png|sub|link=Ashe#Dynamite|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#Dynamite|Dynamite]]. ** Removes attached objects, such as [[File:Magnetic_Grenade.png|sub|link=Cassidy#Magnetic Grenade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Cassidy#Magnetic Grenade|Magnetic Grenade]] and [[File:Ability-hanzo2.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Sonic Arrow|Sonic Arrow]].

abilities.name: Hack

raw.shot type: [[File:Ability target ui.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Targeted ability|Targeted ]] Targeted
raw.official description: Hold to hack. Hacked enemies cannot use abilities and can be seen through walls. Hacked health packs spawn faster and can't be used by enemies. Taking damage interrupts the hack attempt.
raw.duration:
8 seconds (reveal, heroes)
1.0 seconds (ability disable, heroes)
45 seconds (health packs)
10 seconds (disable, objects)
3 seconds (disable, B.O.B.)
raw.ability details: * Hack can target [[heroes]], [[health pack]]s and most enemy [[objects]]. * Sombra can see the ult status of hacked enemies (above their healthbar). * Hack goes on a shorter 3 second cooldown if a non-player unit is hacked or if Hack is interrupted by taking damage. ** If Hack is interrupted without taking any damage, it does not go on cooldown. * Hack cannot be used on an enemy that is already hacked. This refers to the duration of the reveal debuff rather than the ability lock. * Using Hack on enemy objects or health packs doesn't end Stealth, but instead reveals Sombra to nearby enemies. ** Using Hack on enemy heroes will exit Stealth, however. * An unobstructed line of sight to the target is required for the entirety of the startup animation. [[Barrier]]s break line of sight instantly. Walls also break line of sight, but there is a brief grace period where Hack can continue. * Hacked heroes cannot activate their [[Ability|abilities]] for 1 second and are revealed to Sombra's team for 8 seconds. ** A "HACKED" text will appear to the players while their abilities are locked. ** Hack disables all hero-specific [[movement]] [[Passive ability|passive abilities]]. Heroes that have a higher base movement speed keep their increased speed. ** Hack completely turns off [[Lúcio]]'s [[File:Ability-Lucio2.png|sub|link=Lúcio#Crossfade|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lúcio#Crossfade|Crossfade]], retracts [[Sigma]]'s [[File:Abilities-sigma2.png|sub|link=Sigma#Experimental Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Experimental Barrier|Experimental Barrier]] and [[Wrecking Ball]] exits [[File:Roll.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Roll|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Roll|Roll]]. ** Hack does not affect [[weapon]]s nor passive abilities tied to weapons. ** Hacked heroes display a check mark above their head if they have their [[ultimate ability]] available. * Hacked [[Health Pack]]s respawn four times faster — 2.5 seconds for small Health Packs, 3.75 seconds for mega Health Packs — and can be seen through walls to all allies within 30 meters. Hacked Health Packs cannot be used by the enemy team unless their area effected by [[File:Perk_SonicDisruption.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Sonic Disruption|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Sonic Disruption|Sonic Disruption]]. ** Healing from hacked Health Packs is credited to Sombra in the scoreboard, but she does not gain any [[ultimate charge]] from it. ** Health Packs cannot be hacked before the round starts. ** Sombra cannot hack Health Packs that have already been hacked by an enemy Sombra or Hanzo with [[File:Perk_SonicDisruption.png|sub|link=Hanzo#Sonic Disruption|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Hanzo#Sonic Disruption|Sonic Disruption]]. * Following [[object]]s can be hacked, either disabling them briefly or completely destroying them. ** Torbjörn's [[File:Ability-torbjorn3.png|sub|link=Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Deploy Turret|Turret]] and Illari's [[File:Healing_pylon_icon.png|sub|link=Illari#Healing Pylon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Illari#Healing Pylon|Healing Pylon]] are disabled for 10 seconds when hacked. ** Ashe's [[File:Ability-ashe4.png|sub|link=Ashe#B.O.B.|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Ashe#B.O.B.|B.O.B.]] is disabled for 3 seconds when hacked. ** Hacking Lifeweaver's [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] prevents it from activating or causes it to fall on ground, destroying it, if already activated. ** Hacking Baptiste's [[File:Baptiste-ability3.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Immortality Field|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Immortality Field|Immortality Field]] completely destroys it. ** Some objects, such as [[Symmetra]]'s [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turrets]] or [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]], cannot be targeted by Hack, but can still be hacked with [[File:Ability Sombra EMP.png|sub|link=Sombra#EMP|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#EMP|EMP]], refer to [[Objects#List of objects]] for a complete list. * Non-heroes that are hacked have a skull above them that drains over time. This represents the duration of the hack. * Hacked can be completely removed by Zarya's [[File:Ability-zarya3.png|sub|link=Zarya#Projected Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Projected Barrier|Particle/Projected Barrier]], Zenyatta's [[File:Ability-zenyatta4.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Transcendence|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Transcendence|Transcendence]] or abilities that grant [[phased out]] status, such as [[File:Ability-reaper2.png|sub|link=Reaper#Wraith Form|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Reaper#Wraith Form|Wraith Form]] and [[File:Protection_Suzu_Icon.png|sub|link=Kiriko#Protection Suzu|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Kiriko#Protection Suzu|Protection Suzu]]. Becoming phased out while casting hack also prevents it from finishing. * Becoming [[Unstoppable]], such as through [[File:Overrun_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Overrun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Overrun|Overrun]] or [[File:Ability Orisa Fortify.png|sub|link=Orisa#Fortify|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Fortify|Fortify]], prevents Hack's ability lockdown, but not its reveal or [[File:Ability Sombra Opportunist.png|sub|link=Sombra#Opportunist|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Opportunist|Opportunist]] damage boosting.

raw.ability details: * EMP is line of sight blocked by terrain. ** Mei's [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] blocks line of sight to enemies behind it. ** Lifeweaver's [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] and [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] do not block EMP. * EMP completely destroys ''all'' barriers, including [[File:Photon Barrier.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Photon Barrier|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Photon Barrier|Photon Barrier]], Baptiste's [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], and Domina's [[File:ULT Panopticon.png|sub|link=Domina#Panopticon|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Domina#Panopticon|Panopticon]]. ** If [[Zarya]] projects a barrier on a target with shields (e.g. herself), the barrier protects the health and the player will not be hacked. Zarya will gain the [[File:Passive-Zarya.png|sub|link=Zarya#Energy|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Energy|Energy]] from the damage absorbed. * In addition to the [[objects]] that can be hacked by [[File:Ability Sombra Hack.png|sub|link=Sombra#Hack|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sombra#Hack|Hack]], EMP also hacks Symmetra's [[File:Ability-symmetra2.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Sentry Turret|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Sentry Turret|Sentry Turrets]] and [[File:Ability-symmetra4.png|sub|link=Symmetra#Teleporter|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Symmetra#Teleporter|Teleporter]], Mei's [[File:Ability-mei4.png|sub|link=Mei#Blizzard|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Blizzard|Blizzard]] and Wrecking Ball's [[File:Minefield.png|sub|link=Wrecking Ball#Minefield|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Wrecking Ball#Minefield|Proximity Mines]], disabling them for 10 seconds. They must be within Sombra's line of sight, ignoring barriers. Refer to [[Objects#List of objects]] for a complete list. ** Some objects, such as [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]], cannot be hacked but may still be damaged by EMP. ** Mei's [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]] is completely unaffected by EMP. * Unlike other [[Ultimate ability|ultimate abilities]] Sombra alerts her entire team regardless of distance from her that she had used her EMP. This is done by a radio transmission sounding "EMP activated." * Sombra can start gaining ultimate charge after 5 seconds of using EMP. * Sombra receives a small vertical boost from activating EMP.

raw.official description: Create two temporary teleporters that enable instance travel between them.
raw.ability details: * Requires the player to use the Interact key to activate, unless it's an entity separate from a player, in which case it will teleport automatically. (Turrets, [[D.Va]]'s MEKA when self-destructing, etc) ** Teleporter is prioritized over most other actions that require using the Interaction key. * Cannot be used while in spawn. * Can be manually destroyed by pressing the ability key again, including while dead. * If the entrance and exit are more than 40 meters apart, they are automatically destroyed. * After Teleporting, there is a 1 second cooldown before it can be reused again for that same player. * You can hold down the interaction button before the teleport appears and teleport as soon as possible. * Teleporter can be placed on and lifted by [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] regardless of which was deployed first. raw.ability details: * Damage per second: 80 while firing at full charge (71.43 w/ reload) * If the center of the projectile circle does not touch the wall, then the projectile will not explode against the wall. This allows you to shoot at enemies that are located at the edges of shelters. * The shots can be charged to increase their damage, projectile radius and maximum explosion radius. ** A shot takes 1 second to fully charge, and can be held for an additional 1 second upon reaching maximum charge before automatically firing. ** Charge can be canceled by using any ability, including just entering the targeting mode. Any ammo that went to charging the shot is consumed. ** The damage is scaled off charge time, not ammo consumption. Minimum of 1 ammo will be consumed. ** As long as there is any ammo left, an energy ball can still be fired at full charge. * The projectile can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but the explosion is unaffected. * [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]] affects all damage dealt, if the projectile passes through it. Splash damage alone going through is not amplified.

raw.ability details:
*Symmetra does not gain Ultimate charge while the Shield Generator is active.
*The shield generator ignores line-of-sight, meaning that it can be placed in a concealed position or behind cover while still providing the full shield effect.
*It can be interchanged with Teleporter using the Ultimate key before deployment.
*The generator is refunded if there is a change in objective (e.g. point captured/lost).
*It can be destroyed just like the teleporter
*Strangely, newer heros such as [[Juno]] have voice lines for destroying a "Shield Generator" despite it not being in the game since OW1

raw.official description: Overload upgrades your Turret for 5 seconds, increasing its health and damage.
raw.ability details:
* The turret must be placed before Overload is activated to take effect.
* The turret has a 2 second upgrade delay during which it cannot fire.
* If Overload is activated when the turret is being deployed, it will start upgrading once it has been constructed.
* The duration is counted from the activation of Overload, meaning that upgrade time included the actual uptime of the upgraded turret is only 3 seconds.
* The upgraded turret gains +150 health and in addition to its regular fire, it shoots a burst of 3 missiles every 1.5 seconds each missile dealing 30 damage.

raw.ability details:
* ''Removed upon the rework on October 9, 2018.''
* The turret automatically fires at nearby enemies, filling [[Molten Core|Torbjörn's Ultimate]] as it does so. 
* The turret has three upgrade levels:
** A Level 1 turret has 150 health and one gun barrel. Five hits from the Forge Hammer will upgrade it to Level 2.
** A Level 2 turret has 300 health and two gun barrels.
** A Level 3 turret has 800 health, two gun barrels and a rocket launcher. This upgrade is only active during [[Molten Core]].
* The turret's HP must be full for it to be upgraded. If it takes damage, it must be repaired first.
** An exception to this is when Molten Core is activated on a Level 2 turret. All health is restored.

raw.ability details: * Damage per second: 48 * Turret deploy can be thrown to a maximum distance of 15 meters on ground level. * Turret automatically attacks the closest enemy within line of slight and continues attacking that enemy until it is either dead or out of sight. ** Target can be changed if the player damages said enemy with [[Rivet Gun]]'s primary fire. * Turret can be manually destroyed using the Interact key, which instantly inflicts 10,000 damage to it. This can be done while Torbjörn is dead. ** Cannot be done while the Turret is building itself. * Turret cannot be redeployed if it has dealt or received damage within the last 3 seconds. The Deploy Turret key will appear colored to indicate this. ** If the Turret is destroyed during this time, including by the Interact key, Deploy Turret will go on a longer cooldown. * The Turret projectile is not affected by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]] or [[File:Baptiste-ability4.png|sub|link=Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Baptiste#Amplification Matrix|Amplification Matrix]], but its shots interact normally with these effects. * Turret can be placed on [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] regardless of which was deployed first. When the platform is destroyed, the turret falls on ground. * The projectiles destroys fences and other breakables it collides with while in flight.

raw.ability details: * Torbjörn's weapon is temporarily replaced by his claw, which shoots out balls of molten slag. * The projectiles bounce from walls, and upon landing on ground create pools that deal damage over time. * Against armor, the pools deal 290 damage per second. * Damage from pools do not stack: two pools in the same area deal the same damage as one pool. * The projectiles can be destroyed while in air by [[File:Ability-dva3.png|sub|link=D.Va#Defense Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[D.Va#Defense Matrix|Defense Matrix]], [[File:Abilities-sigma3.png|sub|link=Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Kinetic Grasp|Kinetic Grasp]], [[File:Ability_JavelinSpin.png|sub|link=Orisa#Javelin Spin|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Orisa#Javelin Spin|Javelin Spin]], [[File:Perk_PowerMatrix.png|sub|link=Doomfist#Power Matrix|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Doomfist#Power Matrix|Power Matrix]] and [[File:ULT Kekkai Sancturary.png|sub|link=Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mizuki#Kekkai Sanctuary|Kekkai Sanctuary]] and reflected by [[File:Ability-genji2.png|sub|link=Genji#Deflect|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Genji#Deflect|Deflect]], but the pools are unaffected. * The impact damage from Molten Core can be affected by [[damage amplification]], but the damage over time cannot. * [[File:Ability-torbjorn1.png|middle|link=Torbjörn#Rivet Gun|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Torbjörn#Rivet Gun|Rivet Gun]]'s ammo is replenished upon activation.

raw.official description: Travel back in time to your previous location and health.
raw.ability details:
*Also fully reloads Tracer's Pulse Pistols.
*Restores Tracer's health to the highest value in the past 3 seconds.
*Recall does not restore overhealth, but any overhealth Tracer currently has will persist for its normal duration through Recall.
*If Recalling to a position on a platform (or the payload) that has moved during the last few seconds, Tracer will not be Recalled to a position in the air, but instead she will be returned to the platform's current location.
*Recall also records where Tracer was looking three seconds prior.
*Tracer is [[phased out]] while Recalling, removing most negative [[status effect]]s and making her immune to all damage.

abilities.name: Warding Stance

raw.official description: Block with your sword, reducing frontal damage and deflecting melee attacks. Blocking damage depletes energy.
raw.ability details: * Can block 250 damage maximum, which chunks 62.5hp off your health bar * Only depletes energy when taking damage, but energy does not regenerate while Warding Stance is up. * Energy cost is based on the raw damage of the attack, before any damage reductions. * Must have at least 30% energy remaining to start blocking, however dropping below 30% while already blocking will not end the ability. * When an instance of damage reduces energy to zero, it will still be reduced in full. * It takes ~11.1 seconds for Warding Stance to fully recharge. * The lines in the energy bar correspond to 30% and 60% of max energy. * Using any abilities, except [[File:Quick melee.png|sub|link=Melee#Quick Melee|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Melee#Quick Melee|Quick Melee]], will interrupt blocking.

Each stage increases the damage and area of the slash. ** Once stage 3 has been reached, spending more time charging has no benefit. ** Will automatically fire when 4 seconds have expired. * Vendetta is not affected by gravity while charging. ** When complete, Vendetta will fall faster than gravity and the cleave will hit the ground. * Secondary fire provides a small camera angle change. * Sundering Blade counts as an Overhead Strike for cases like Siphoning Strike that grant lifesteal.

raw.ability details: * Grants ''additional'' +300% cooldown refresh rate, meaning that cooldowns tick at 4x speed.

raw.official description: Using abilities grants temporary shields.
raw.ability details:
* Total amount of [[shield]] health gained is capped at 75. 
** Using multiple will stack shield health, but not exceed the limit.
* Shields are granted when the ability successfully starts. E.g. in case of Burrow, upon entering underground.
* Clobber does not trigger Explorer's Resolve. (Covered in Dirt Major perk only)
* If Venture takes damage, the shields start regenerating after 1.1 seconds at a rate of 19 shields per second.
* Shields start decaying at the end of the duration at a rate of 30 shield health per second.
** Using an ability resets the decay timer.
** Shield decay timer is paused while using Burrow or Tectonic Shock.
* Shield gain is not prevented by [[anti-heal]] effects.
* Shields are recoverable [[hit points]], which means damage taken by them can be healed until they decay.

shot_type: [[File:Swift_Step_Icon.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Movement ability|Movement ]] Movement [[File:Ability-parah3.png|40x40px|sub|class=inline-icon invert-image|link=Area of effect|Area of effect ]] Area of effect

raw.official description: Move underground and become invulnerable. Emerge to deal damage.
raw.cast time:

raw.radius:
2 - 7 meters (max charge, falloff start - max)
1 - 3.5 meters (min charge, falloff start - max)
raw.ability details: * Reveals enemy health bars without having dealt damage to them. * Cooldown starts when the ability finishes casting. * Venture receives a [[movement speed]] penalty while casting, replaced by a movement speed buff once underground. * Pressing primary fire, jump or the ability button again causes Venture to emerge from underground, dealing damage and [[knockback|knocking back]] enemies in the area. ** Holding the button allows Venture to charge the ability, increasing the emerging area, damage dealt and leap speed. ** There is a 0.2 second grace period when the ability's duration ends where emerging can still be charged. * Burrow will replenish Smart Excavator's ammo once underground. * Removes most negative [[status effect]]s and unsticks projectiles when successfully going underground. * While burrowed, Venture can drop down ledges, but only travel up through ramps. * Burrowed Venture cannot pass through [[File:Ability-mei3.png|sub|link=Mei#Ice Wall|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Ice Wall|Ice Wall]], [[File:Tree_of_Life_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Tree of Life|Tree of Life]] or [[File:Ability-mei2.png|sub|link=Mei#Cryo-Freeze|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mei#Cryo-Freeze|Cryo-Freeze]]. * While underground, Venture cannot be targeted or hit by [[heroes]] or [[object]]s, both allied and enemy. ** When dropping down a ledge, Venture briefly loses the invulnerability and can be targeted and hit by allied and enemy effects. * When an [[File:Ability-zenyatta2.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Orb of Harmony|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Orb of Harmony|Orb of Harmony]] has been applied to Venture earlier, they keep it while underground until they break line of sight. However, [[File:Ability-zenyatta3.png|sub|link=Zenyatta#Orb of Discord|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zenyatta#Orb of Discord|Orb of Discord]] is instantly removed, and [[File:Ability-mercy1.png|sub|link=Mercy#Caduceus Staff|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mercy#Caduceus Staff|Caduceus Staff]] is removed after a short period. * When emerging, the damage is dealt before Venture launches up and can destroy mines and traps without harming Venture. * Venture cannot activate traps, elevators or [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] while underground. * If venture starts emerge-charging on [[File:Petal_Platform_Icon.png|sub|link=Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Lifeweaver#Petal Platform|Petal Platform]] , and Lifeweaver cancels the platform, Venture's burrow will be force-cancelled.'"`UNIQ--ref-0000005E-QINU`"''"`UNIQ--ref-0000005F-QINU`"' ** Lifeweaver can use this technique to quickly place and cancel petal above a burrowed emerge-charging Venture, force-cancelling burrow. * [[File:Cage_Fight_icon.png|sub|link=Mauga#Cage Fight|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Mauga#Cage Fight|Cage Fight]], [[File:Ability-zarya4.png|sub|link=Zarya#Graviton Surge|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Zarya#Graviton Surge|Graviton Surge]] and [[File:Abilities-sigma5.png|sub|link=Sigma#Gravitic Flux|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Sigma#Gravitic Flux|Gravitic Flux]] do not affect Venture if they are underground. However, these abilities will prevent activating Burrow if Venture is caught in one of them, and can interrupt Burrow if Venture exposes themself by dropping down a ledge in their area. * Venture can't capture or push objectives while they're underground. They can however contest objectives while underground, such as to prevent enemy progress or stall overtime. * Venture can pick up [[health pack]]s while underground. * Burrow's [[movement speed]] buff does not count towards global movement speed buff cap. * Destroys fences and other breakables in the way when traveling underground.

duration.raw:
4 seconds (burrowed)
0.2 seconds (emerging, min charge)

raw.official description: Leap forward into the air. Landing on an enemy damages them.
raw.ability details: * Cooldown starts immediately after activation. * The damage scales based on distance to target upon landing. * Enemies that collide with Winston mid-leap take 1 damage. * Enemies that are hit by the impact or collide with Winston mid-leap are knocked back. * Being stunned or slept temporarily prevents the landing effects from applying, but does not fully interrupt the leap. In other words, the landing damage and knockback will apply as long as the negative effects expire before he lands. * Winston can use (and charge) Tesla Cannon, melee and [[File:Ability-winston3.png|sub|link=Winston#Barrier Projector|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Barrier Projector|Barrier Projector]] and [[File:Ability-winston4.png|sub|link=Winston#Primal Rage|40x40px |class=inline-icon invert-image]] [[Winston#Primal Rage|Primal Rage]] while leaping. * After using Jump Pack, a standard melee attack right before landing will cancel the melee animation while the damage of the attack is still applied. While the visual animation of the melee is cancelled, Winston still is unable to use his Tesla Cannon until 0.5 seconds after he used the melee attack. This makes using melee right before landing no more or less efficient than interrupting the Tesla Cannon with melee attacks while grounded. * View angle and use of directional inputs affect the jump length. ** To perform particularly short jumps with the Jump Pack, look straight ahead and press and hold backwards right before using the ability. To jump straight up, look straight up and press and hold backwards right before jumping.
